subject: fix(grip-generation): honour $CLAUDE_DIR so non-default installs resolve real Gen N (#3646)
what: Fixed honour $CLAUDE_DIR so non-default installs resolve real Gen N (#3646).
why:
* fix(grip-generation): honour $CLAUDE_DIR so non-default installs resolve real Gen N

resolve_generation hardcoded CLAUDE_DIR = ~/.claude, so a GRIP install at a non-default path (e.g. ~/.grip, container/CI) read a non-existent ~/.claude index and fell back to "new project (no GRIP history here)" in the warm-start header even with 100+ instances at a real Gen N in its own project index.

Route install-dir resolution through the canonical claude_dir.get_claude_dir() shim (rules/path-portability.md), bootstrapping sys.path from __file__ so the shim import never depends on the value it resolves. Existing tests unaffected (they patch the module constant); adds a CLI-driven regression that fails under the hardcode mutation.

Verified: ~/.grip resolves a real Gen N instead of "new project (no GRIP history here)".

Eval: tests/test_grip_generation.py 8/8 (100%)

* fix(grip-generation): make claude_dir-shim-missing fallback loud + tested

Address AGORA #3646 Speaker C kernel: the except-ImportError fallback to ~/.claude was silent. Emit a stderr diagnostic so a genuinely-absent core shim is never masked, and document in-code that a SyntaxError in claude_dir.py is NOT caught (it propagates, so a code defect is never hidden). Adds test_fallback_is_loud_when_shim_missing (forces the ImportError via a sys.modules swap + reload).

R0 dispositions on the other concerns: _LIB-undefined is false (judge-refuted; 9/9 tests pass); self-referential-import is speculative (claude_dir is a sibling module); the guarded sys.path insert is the pre-existing path-portability idiom, not introduced here.

Eval: tests/test_grip_generation.py 9/9 (100%)

---------

subject: feat(overnight-loop): launchd lean-wave autonomous overnight grinder (#3650)
what: Added launchd lean-wave autonomous overnight grinder (#3650).
why:
A terminal-close-surviving overnight loop. launchd fires bin/grip-overnight-wave
every ~20 min; each wave dispatches ONE bounded claude -p step that advances the
sprint-loop queue (merge a green PR, fix a red one, or start the next issue) with
the WORK-state living in GitHub PRs, not agent memory. That is the fix for the
2026-06-21 failure where a single background agent died at one context envelope
having shipped nothing: here a dead wave loses nothing and the next wave reads
current state and continues.

Pieces:
- lib/overnight_loop.py: pure should_fire() gate (enabled + within-deadline + no
  STOP + no overlapping wave), parse_until, cfg I/O, wave_argv. 10 Goodhart-proof
  tests (each gate flips a controlled case).
- bin/grip-overnight-wave: launchd-fired wrapper: atomic dir-lock with stale-lock
  steal, yolo refresh, dispatch, log to logs/overnight-loop.jsonl. Native keychain
  auth (no token handling, no leak surface).
- bin/grip-overnight: control CLI on --until 8h / off / stop / status; installs +
  loads the launchd plist.
- config/overnight-wave-prompt.md: lean bounded-step prompt + fixed safety rails
  (no force-push/prod/destructive, bootstrap-human, no-vapourware,
  concurrent-commit-plumbing, pragmatic auto-merge).
- config/com.grip.overnight-loop.plist: launchd reference (StartInterval 1200,
  RunAtLoad, gated so a disabled loop no-ops on fire).

Verified: 10/10 unit tests; gate skips when disabled (no claude launched); plist
plutil-lint OK; live dry-run wave fired claude -p (rc=0, 149s), correctly reasoned
the next step (#3523 over runners-up #3548/#3549) and changed nothing. Default
OFF; grip-overnight on --until 8h starts it.

subject: fix(kono): degrade store pipeline gracefully when embedding backend is down (#3651)
what: Fixed degrade store pipeline gracefully when embedding backend is down (#3651).
why:
* fix(kono): degrade store pipeline gracefully when embedding backend is down

The KONO session-summary step of /save crashed when the ollama embedding daemon was offline: store_embedding -> embed_text -> _ensure_model raised RuntimeError('Ollama daemon unreachable') (a ConnectionRefusedError underneath), which escaped _run_store_pipeline unhandled and failed the step with a traceback. Root cause was the daemon being down, NOT a Python 3.14 issue (the stdlib paths in the traceback were misleading). _run_store_pipeline now wraps the embedding-dependent ops (get_engine + store_embedding + mine) in the same graceful-degradation guard the hybrid-search read path already uses: log debug + return None (the documented 'None on failure'). The CLI handler already prints a clean status and exits 0 on None. Adds a mutation-sensitive regression test.

Primary Author: miaroos

* fix(kono): restore default namespace to enter-konsult (drop accidentally-swept change)

The prior commit's plumbing update-index swept an unrelated working-tree edit (default namespace enter-konsult->example-co at store/retrieve/_resolve_accessible_namespaces) into the diff. AGORA correctly flagged it as a memory-orphaning regression for deployments with GRIP_ORG unset. This restores the namespace defaults to origin/main (enter-konsult), keeping ONLY the graceful-degradation guard. Net PR diff = the guard + its regression test.

Primary Author: miaroos

* fix(kono): narrow degradation guard to backend ops so mine() faults surface

AGORA PR #3651 iteration (Speaker C): the prior guard wrapped mine() + the counter inside the try, so a genuine mine() fault after a successful store_embedding would be masked as backend-unavailability and skip the counter. Narrowed the try to ONLY the backend ops (get_engine + store_embedding), mirroring the hybrid-search read path; mine() + counter now run after the guard so real faults surface. Adds a 2nd mutation-proof regression test (store_embedding succeeds + mine() raises -> RuntimeError propagates). Namespace defaults stay origin/main's enter-konsult (0 example-co).

Primary Author: miaroos

subject: fix(gates): re-enable is_destructive_git detection (bypass stays at orchestrator layer, V>>--<<V)
what: Fixed re-enable is_destructive_git detection (bypass stays at orchestrator layer, V>>--<<V).
why: The 2026-04-02 disable returned None unconditionally, crippling destructive-git detection for ALL sessions (not just RSI sprints) — the autonomous bypass was in the wrong layer. bash_orchestrator._run_destructive_git already gates the deny behind is_autonomous_bypass_fn(gate), allowing the op for yolo/RSI loops + explicit approval. So detection belongs in the function; enforcement + bypass stay in the orchestrator. Re-enabled real detection over DESTRUCTIVE_GIT_PATTERNS (force-push, hard-reset, working-tree-wipe, discard-checkout/restore, stash-drop). The stale test_destructive_git_detection now passes (27/27 in test_core_features). Interactive destructive ops are gated again (proper posture); autonomous loops unblocked via the existing bypass. V>>--<<V ratified 2026-06-24. Resolves #31.

why: Capture loop activation (wf_c09a12a2, anti-theatre gated). The hook is PostToolUse(Bash) so WIRING IS THE TRIGGER (no launchd — a timer cannot observe an in-session Bash result; that would only serve the deferred proposer). D1: tests/test_bash_error_capture_e2e.py drives a real failing-bash payload through hooks/bash_error_learner_hook.py and asserts a row lands in a CLAUDE_DIR-redirected insights store (Goodhart-proof: fails if the hook is disconnected, classify() returns None, or the learn-event import regresses). D2: wire bash_error_learner_hook.py into config/settings.reference.json PostToolUse Bash (canonical install path; install.sh --update regenerates settings.json; never hand-edited). Now a shell mistake GRIP makes in any future session is classified + persisted autonomously. V>>--<<V ratified 2026-06-24.

subject: feat(learning): RSI proposer + harness-agnostic orchestrate shim (banach-folded, IDR-signed, V>>--<<V)
what: Added rSI proposer + harness-agnostic orchestrate shim (banach-folded, IDR-signed, V>>--<<V).
why: V>>-ratified build of the anti-theatre-deferred pieces (#35), real now that capture is wired+flowing. lib/learning_proposer.py: reads bash_execution_error captures from learning/insights.jsonl, groups recurring patterns, proposes ONE minimal fix per group, routes to learning/pending/ (status=pending_review) + a GitHub-issue body for human/AGORA ratification — NEVER self-applies (exogenous-anchor-law). Banach fixed-point: propose-after-propose==propose (dedup id=sha256(category|signature)[:16], order-independent); selftest fails loudly if idempotency breaks. Each proposal emits a signed IDR via lib/precog/idr.py (HMAC-SHA256, round-trip VERIFIED). lib/orchestrate.py: harness-agnostic shim (native CC TaskSpec + portable HAL infer), first consumer = proposer. Proven on LIVE data: 2 captures -> 1 proposal. 27 tests, CC<=13.

Fixed the script-vs-module import (path-portability idiom). 10 tests. The ~4x/day schedule + post-activation is the one HITL for V>.

(lib.precog.idr.read_idr_chain) + render each decision's why via the existing
lib.decision_record.render_plain (decision_anatomy), with the predecessor linkage as

PUBLIC site) — this is the in-session introspection read.

subject: fix(chiefofstaff): live answers — route chat to plain /api/infer + fold context into prompt
what: Fixed live answers — route chat to plain /api/infer + fold context into prompt.

/api/infer-with-tools, whose CCH/Anthropic tool-loop raises on the groq chat
model. Fixes:

  holistic-kernel + KONO context is folded INTO the prompt. Behaviour proven: the
  model now grounds in context instead of hallucinating.
- HAL URLs env-overridable (CHIEFOFSTAFF_HAL_URL / _PLAIN_URL) for deploy + a
  hermetic e2e (dead URL -> fast fail-soft). 22 tests pass.

why:

content-address vs signature, Merkle forest, external anchoring) with a real

the IDR.md example's content_addr via the real lib/precog/idr.py (4 passing).

subject: feat(opencode): safety-gate bridge — run GRIP's hard-DENY gates under OpenCode (#3713)
what: Added safety-gate bridge — run GRIP's hard-DENY gates under OpenCode (#3713).
why:
Under OpenCode, GRIP's Claude-Code PreToolUse safety gates never fire, so the
hard DENYs (secrets/PII, hollow stubs, cyclomatic complexity, DRY/KISS, cross-org
leak, cross-repo writes, channel scope, MEMORY clobber) are silently off. This
bridges them so the same gates protect OpenCode tool calls.

subject: feat(discord): grip-discord-voice conversational follow-up + Whisper-noise filter (#3718)
what: Added grip-discord-voice conversational follow-up + Whisper-noise filter (#3718).
why:
Once a speaker addresses GRIP, a 25s follow-up window lets them continue WITHOUT
re-saying the wake word (same speaker only). Whisper silence hallucinations
(thank you / gracias / bye / sub-3-char) are filtered so they cannot hijack the
window or trigger a reply. Wake word fuzzy-matched for common STT mishears (krip/crip).

subject: feat(discord): grip-discord-voice gated machine control (--act, passphrase-armed) (#3721)
what: Added grip-discord-voice gated machine control (--act, passphrase-armed) (#3721).
why:
Opt-in machine control for the voice bot, ratified V>>--<<V 2026-06-26. OFF by default.
Speak a secret passphrase (keychain grip-voice-passphrase, read in-process, never in
the repo) to arm + lock control to that speaker. Safe actions run fluidly via the real
claude harness with its safety gates INTACT (never --dangerously-skip); irreversible
actions (delete/push/deploy/send) are read back for a spoken confirm. 'grip full stop'
is a deterministic brake. Audit-logged to logs/grip-voice-actions.jsonl. Pure
classifyUtterance() is the tested safety surface.

subject: docs: reconcile stale capability counts + activate-cips->activate-grip (#3722)
what: Documented reconcile stale capability counts + activate-cips->activate-grip (#3722).

One consistent inventory set across README.md, .claude/CLAUDE.md, and
rules/system-capabilities.md, pinned to the wired generator lib/asset_counter.py
(skills/*/SKILL.md; *.md minus CLAUDE.md for agents/commands/rules; hooks/*.py;
modes/definitions/*.yaml): 296 skills / 41 agents / 97 commands / 47 rules /
88 hooks / 31 modes.

subject: fix(discord): grip-discord-voice passphrase arming robust to Whisper STT drift (#3723)
what: Fixed grip-discord-voice passphrase arming robust to Whisper STT drift (#3723).
why:
The arm-check sat behind the wake-gate, so a mis-transcribed GRIP (RIP/Krip) in the
passphrase killed arming before it ran; exact-substring matching was also too brittle
for speech. Now the arm-check runs BEFORE the wake-gate (the passphrase is self-
authorising) and phraseMatch() fuzzy-matches >=70% of the secret's non-wake tokens IN
ORDER (tolerant of grip->krip and a dropped tail word); random speech still cannot arm.

subject: feat(memory): generation-bridge line in compact-brief emission (#3720)
what: Added generation-bridge line in compact-brief emission (#3720).
why:
format_compact_brief links each generation to its nearest distinct
predecessor (Gen N <- Gen pred) when one exists, making the memory chain
explicit and mirroring the IDR hash-chain. Predecessor derived from the
displayed achievement timeline (newest distinct generation strictly older
than current), never arithmetic gen-1 (timeline dedups by text/skips
checkpoints). Single/first-generation sessions keep the plain Gen line.

subject: feat(discord): grip-discord-voice armed-conversational actions (no repeated wake word) (#3724)
what: Added grip-discord-voice armed-conversational actions (no repeated wake word) (#3724).
why:
Once armed, the operator issues actions conversationally - a leading action verb (after
optional filler like okay/now) fires WITHOUT re-saying grip, so 'okay now create a
program that memoises X' executes. Only on the armed+operator path; questions and
non-imperatives stay chat; risky verbs still require a spoken confirm. phraseMatch arm
+ safeword brake unchanged.

subject: fix(discord): grip-discord-voice action spawn uses --permission-mode acceptEdits (#3725)
what: Fixed grip-discord-voice action spawn uses --permission-mode acceptEdits (#3725).
why:
Machine-control actions that write files timed out (spawnSync claude ETIMEDOUT): the
headless claude -p had no permission to use tools, so a write task hung on an
unanswerable interactive prompt. The spawn now passes --permission-mode acceptEdits
(edits proceed; GRIP mechanical PreToolUse DENY gates STILL fire, independent of
permission mode - guardrails intact; NOT --dangerously-skip-permissions). Timeout
180s->300s, maxBuffer 4->8MB for real coding tasks.

subject: feat(ultraclear): auto HOT-safe /clear + retire focus-card session hooks (#3727)
what: Added auto HOT-safe /clear + retire focus-card session hooks (#3727).
why:
A bare /clear is a harness window-reset, not a compaction, so it dropped the
verbatim HOT working set and a fresh session woke disoriented. Claude Code has
no pre-clear hook and /clear bypasses UserPromptSubmit, so /clear cannot be
intercepted; instead keep the HOT manifest continuously fresh so /clear is
always safe by the time it fires.

subject: feat(chiefofstaff): sign a one-tap iPhone Shortcut (kills the manual hand-build) (#3719)
what: Added sign a one-tap iPhone Shortcut (kills the manual hand-build) (#3719).
why:
grip-cos only printed config for a 5-action manual build — the exact step
Craig got stuck on. Add lib/cos_shortcut_gen.py: emits the WFWorkflow plist
via plistlib (valid-by-construction), wires the magic-variable chain
(Dictate Text -> POST {"text"} to /respond with Bearer auth -> Get
Dictionary Value "reply" -> Speak Text), and signs it (shortcuts sign
--mode anyone -> AEA1) into a one-tap-import Chief-of-Staff.shortcut.

subject: feat(crypto): Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65 dual signature primitive (D2 key custody) (#3726)
what: Added ed25519 + ML-DSA-65 dual signature primitive (D2 key custody) (#3726).
why:
Phase-1 key-custody for the cryptographic-causation moat. D2 locked by V>>
2026-06-26: records that leave the machine or back an outward claim are
dual-signed - classical Ed25519 + post-quantum ML-DSA-65 (FIPS 204) - so a
record stays verifiable even if one primitive is later broken.

subject: feat(crypto): promote shadow completeness to live authority (Phase-1 step 2) (#3733)
what: Added promote shadow completeness to live authority (Phase-1 step 2) (#3733).
why:
Shadow completeness commitment promoted to LIVE:
- context_snapshot._paramount_commitment: shadow=True -> live=True
- context_snapshot.verify_paramount_completeness: 7 deterministic steps
  (chain_loaded, integrity_verified, paramount_identified,
   merkle_root_computed, previous_root_loaded,
   monotonicity_checked, root_committed)
- Each step returns exact pass/fail for audit/testability
- Empty chain is vacuously complete (genesis admissible)
- Monotone-N floor enforced: decreased paramount_n = step 6 fail

subject: feat(crypto): dual-chain anchoring shim — Bitcoin OTS + Sepolia (Phase-1 step 3) (#3735)
what: Added dual-chain anchoring shim — Bitcoin OTS + Sepolia (Phase-1 step 3) (#3735).
why:
One shared anchoring shim across FF + IDR:
- lib/crypto_anchor.py: dual-chain Merkle-root anchoring
  - anchor_dual(): anchor to Bitcoin (OpenTimestamps) AND Sepolia (ff_ops)
  - d4_guard(): REFUSE to anchor any record carrying DONNA/client-data markers
  - OTS: submits SHA-256 digest to OpenTimestamps calendar pools (3 fallback
    servers), saves .ots receipt to state/crypto-anchor/
  - Sepolia: reuses ff_ops.publishRecord via Alchemy CLI
  - Best-effort: single-chain failure does not abort the other chain
- 12 Goodhart-proof tests (D4 guard, OTS submit/fallback, dual anchor, D4 block)

subject: fix(grip-cos): launchd plist PATH + venv python (CoS endpoint 530 root cause) (#3734)
what: Fixed launchd plist PATH + venv python (CoS endpoint 530 root cause) (#3734).
why:
The stable Chief-of-Staff endpoint (cos.grip-web.com) was returning HTTP 530
(Cloudflare tunnel origin unreachable) because the launchd agent crash-looped:
launchd's default PATH omits the Homebrew prefixes, so 'grip-cos serve' could
not find cloudflared and exited 1 every restart. The iPhone shortcut then
parsed Cloudflare's 530 error page instead of JSON -> 'couldn't convert Rich
Text to Dictionary'.

subject: feat(bin): grip-sovereign-demo — parallel non-Anthropic provider proof
what: Added grip-sovereign-demo — parallel non-Anthropic provider proof.
why:
N-way concurrent HAL provider demo over OpenAI-compatible endpoints
(DeepSeek/OpenAI/Gemini/Groq/xAI). Proves provider-independence live: one
prompt answered in parallel by multiple non-Anthropic providers, zero
Anthropic egress, with a wall-clock concurrency check (total < sum) as a
falsifiable H-SOVEREIGN-1 verdict. Keys read in-process from Keychain,
never printed.

subject: feat(bin): grip-sovereign-demo — add Gemini 3.1 Pro + reasoning-model + concurrency-fix
what: Added grip-sovereign-demo — add Gemini 3.1 Pro + reasoning-model + concurrency-fix.

- Handle reasoning-model responses (content absent when the token budget is spent
  thinking; fall back to reasoning_content, report finish_reason, raise max_tokens).
- Fix concurrency verdict: compare recovered overlap to the max achievable (sum-max),
  not total<0.7*sum — old test false-negatived when one provider's latency dominated.

subject: feat(bin): grip-sovereign-council — zero-Anthropic multi-LLM council with tamper-evident seal
what: Added grip-sovereign-council — zero-Anthropic multi-LLM council with tamper-evident seal.
why:
Dogfoods AGORA's exogenous-anchor pattern on a pure non-Anthropic substrate:
N speakers answer in parallel; a DIFFERENT non-Anthropic model judges and
synthesises (exogenous — no model grades its own work); the verdict is sealed
with sha256 over {prompt, answers, verdict}. --verify recomputes -> PROVEN
untampered / TAMPERED on any edit.

subject: docs(rules): parallel-default over either-or (PARAMOUNT)
what: Documented parallel-default over either-or (PARAMOUNT).
why:
When >=2 independent non-exclusive items exist, DO them in parallel (batched
tool calls / Agents / different LLMs via HAL) — never an either/or menu.
'Which one?' only for genuine mutually-exclusive forks. Sharpens Execution-Bias
with the parallelism dimension; bounded by dispatch-economics. Origin V>> 2026-06-27.

subject: feat(opencode): sovereign DeepSeek + OpenAI providers via HAL keychain
what: Added sovereign DeepSeek + OpenAI providers via HAL keychain.
why:
Drop hard dependency on Anthropic P1 gateway (:4010) for non-Anthropic models.
grip-opencode now loads grip-deepseek and grip-openai from keychain and exports
DEEPSEEK_API_KEY / OPENAI_API_KEY so opencode.json direct providers resolve.
HAL gateway key still loaded for hal/claude-* models (optional, soft-fail).

subject: feat(bin): grip-sovereign-council — sign verdicts into the IDR forest
what: Added grip-sovereign-council — sign verdicts into the IDR forest.
why:
Each sealed verdict is now appended as a signed IDR leaf (lib.precog.idr
build_idr + append_idr) anchored under the council: exogenous-anchor
prefix. --verify now reports dual sha256 + IDR HMAC provenance; degrades
gracefully to sha256-only when the lib is unavailable (never a stub).
3 Goodhart-proof tests added (leaf created, verifies, tampered id fails).

subject: feat(opencode): portable sovereign config — launcher self-writes on first run
what: Added portable sovereign config — launcher self-writes on first run.
why:
grip-opencode now writes ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json from a committed repo
template (config/opencode-sovereign.json) on first run — idempotent, never
clobbers a user-edited config. Closes the portability gap: clone GRIP + have the
keychain keys -> a working non-Anthropic OpenCode REPL. Also fixes stale header
comments (openai/gpt-5.5 -> oai/gpt-4o-mini; gpt-5.5 was unverified).
Proven: fresh XDG_CONFIG_HOME -> config auto-written -> deepseek returned PORTABLE-OK.

subject: feat(bin): grip-sovereign-council --merkle — externally-anchorable root over all verdicts
what: Added grip-sovereign-council --merkle — externally-anchorable root over all verdicts.
why:
Closes the 'IDR leaf not Merkle-rooted' gap: --merkle builds an RFC6962 Merkle
root over every sealed council verdict (lib/idr_forest.forest_merkle_root) — one
content-bound commitment that binds all verdicts; submitting it on-chain anchors
the chain to an exogenous timestamp (crypto-causation program). Goodhart-proof
test: root commits N verdicts and changes when a verdict is added. 8 tests pass.

subject: feat(opencode): portable safety gate — link grip-safety.js on first run
what: Added portable safety gate — link grip-safety.js on first run.
why:
Closes the safety half of OpenCode portability: a fresh sovereign OpenCode now
also symlinks integrations/opencode/grip-safety.js into the plugins dir on first
run, so GRIP DENY gates run on any machine. Symlinked so repo gate updates
propagate. Proven: fresh config home -> config + safety plugin both created.

strongest, the genome marches up the page.

Fugu's Ubuntu+macOS-only one-liner. Polyglot one file (bash/python/curl-pipe).

witnessable happiverse node ('dont trust it - witness it') and wires the
creator-approval unlock (bin/grip-unlock + lib/license_handshake.py: dynamic
HMAC nonce/machine/time-bound code, no static secret). Dogfooded: H707 + IDR.

limit is never the bottleneck.

subject: feat(whatsapp): GRIP_CHANNEL_MCP_HOST to route WA egress off-localhost
what: Added gRIP_CHANNEL_MCP_HOST to route WA egress off-localhost.
why: grip-channel WA bridge runs on the Mac (127.0.0.1:3101). VPS grip-channel is Slack+Discord only (whatsapp:false, Hetzner IP blocked at the Baileys handshake), so a VPS-originated WhatsApp send had nowhere to go.

subject: feat(grip-channel): env-gate MCP bind host (GRIP_CHANNEL_MCP_BIND)
what: Added env-gate MCP bind host (GRIP_CHANNEL_MCP_BIND).
why: The daemon hardcoded listen on 127.0.0.1 (index.ts:1033), so the VPS relay (no local WhatsApp bridge, Hetzner IP blocked at the Baileys handshake) could not reach the Mac WA egress over Tailscale. GRIP_CHANNEL_MCP_BIND (default 127.0.0.1, byte-identical when unset) lets the Mac daemon bind the tailnet so the VPS can route WA sends to it. Mirrors the existing GRIP_CHANNEL_MCP_PORT pattern.

subject: fix(relay): honour the outbound STOP brake on relay-originated sends
what: Fixed honour the outbound STOP brake on relay-originated sends.
why: The dispatcher delivers replies via _default_ack -> _mcp_call (channel_reply), a path that never checked STOP_MARKER — only whatsapp_transport.post did. So grip-stop on (the operator safeword, full-power-stop-override.md) did NOT halt relay-originated WhatsApp/Slack/Discord sends: a confirmed STOP-brake bypass.

subject: feat(relay): B-done-safely WhatsApp egress via Mac forced-command SSH
what: Added b-done-safely WhatsApp egress via Mac forced-command SSH.
why: The VPS relay had no way to deliver a WhatsApp reply: its grip-channel is Slack+Discord only (whatsapp:false; Hetzner IP blocked at the Baileys handshake) and the Mac with the live bridge binds 127.0.0.1. The WA transport mesh council (wf_7ab9628f-aac) chose the no-auth, no-tailnet-listener path: the dispatcher SSHes to the Mac and runs a FIXED forced-command egress script with the payload over STDIN.

subject: feat(install): triple-polyglot installer document + content-negotiated UI (#3766)
what: Added triple-polyglot installer document + content-negotiated UI (#3766).
why:
blackbook-xp is now a triple-polyglot — valid bash AND python3 AND readable
markdown. The proven bash+python installer is byte-identical (a strict prefix);
a markdown documentation block is appended in an unused python raw-string after
the final os.execvp, so bash never reaches it and python parses it as a constant.
It reads as a document (bash blackbook-xp.md) while installing unchanged.

subject: chore(relay): ol' trusty WA exit-node setup script (forced-command key install)
what: Maintained ol' trusty WA exit-node setup script (forced-command key install).
why: Idempotent setup run ON ol' trusty (lauries-macbook-pro, [HOST-4]): pulls GRIP, checks the WA bridge + python3, installs the relay's locked-down authorized_keys forced-command (runs ONLY lib/wa_egress_stdin.py), fixes macOS sshd perms, enables Remote Login. AirDrop it or git pull it. Pubkey only (public); the private key stays on the VPS.

subject: feat(grip-channel): /api/send-file route for WhatsApp/Discord document send (#3768)
what: Added /api/send-file route for WhatsApp/Discord document send (#3768).
why:
The local daemon's WhatsApp bridge already has sendDocument() and Discord has
sendFile(), but the control API only exposed text (/api/send). Add POST
/api/send-file {platform, recipient, fileBase64, fileName, mimeType, caption}
so scripts can push PDFs/images/documents through the running bridge (e.g.
install/report PDFs to WhatsApp). Mirrors /api/send: look up the bridge,
base64-decode, dispatch to sendDocument (whatsapp) or sendFile (discord),
record the caption via the external-send hook. stdlib/no new deps.

subject: fix(grip-channel): restore /api/send-file route + ol-trusty setup dropped by #3767 (stale-base clobber) (#3770)
what: Fixed restore /api/send-file route + ol-trusty setup dropped by #3767 (stale-base clobber) (#3770).
why:
PR #3767 (rtk gh pr view filter) was committed from a stale base and silently
reverted two already-merged files it never intended to touch: the /api/send-file
route from #3768 (57469898) and scripts/grip-ol-trusty-wa-setup.sh from 0e5d0264.
The route is live on the local channel daemon and has delivered real documents.
Restore both on top of current HEAD (additive only — #3767 rtk filter work is
untouched). Root cause: a concurrent commit that did not read-tree the live ref
fresh (rules/concurrent-commit-plumbing.md).

subject: fix(rtk): scope gh-pr-view filter to view-only + add gh-pr-diff filter (#3769) (#3772)
what: Fixed scope gh-pr-view filter to view-only + add gh-pr-diff filter (#3769) (#3772).
why: PR #3767 merged a single rtk filter matching both `gh pr view` and `gh pr diff`. Its keep-lines list only preserves PR-view metadata, so a `gh pr diff` matched nothing and collapsed to the `gh pr view: ok` sentinel — every `gh pr diff` silently discarded its diff.

subject: fix(audit-chain): bootstrap sys.path so bare file_lock import resolves
what: Fixed bootstrap sys.path so bare file_lock import resolves.
why:
lib/audit_chain.py did `from file_lock import FileLock` (bare sibling
import, needs lib/ on sys.path) immediately followed by `from
lib.state_manager import ...` (dotted, needs the repo root on sys.path)
-- two mutually exclusive requirements. The standard invocation pattern
used throughout GRIP (PYTHONPATH=$CLAUDE_DIR, repo root only) hit
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'file_lock' on the very first
import, silently breaking every caller that imports this module as
lib.audit_chain -- including this overnight wave's own mandatory IDR +
memory-chain recording step (rules/cryptographic-causation.md).

subject: fix: update-team --post TypeError and force-push gate false-match (#3812, #3804) (#3821)
what: Fixed update-team --post TypeError and force-push gate false-match (#3812, #3804) (#3821).
why:
aurora_report._do_post dropped the plain_language parameter; the
update_team callsite still passed it, so every real --post raised
TypeError while the suite's fake absorbed the kwarg. Pass only declared
kwargs and replace test_update_team_post_requests_plain_language -- which
pinned the dropped kwarg against a fake and so stayed green while runtime
broke -- with a regression test that patches the Slack transport rather
than _do_post itself, so any future signature drift between the callsite
and _do_post fails in CI. Closes #3812.

subject: feat(scope): cross-org/individual scope-contamination kernel (idempotent-no-op proof) (#3827)
what: Added cross-org/individual scope-contamination kernel (idempotent-no-op proof) (#3827).
why:
What this does (plain English): GRIP runs across organisations and
individuals at once. This kernel decides whether any content, memory, or
output is CLEAN for a target (org, individual) scope by proving that
sanitising it is a no-op -- the SAME idempotent-no-op proof git-solve uses
on merge-tree, transplanted to a non-git domain. Clean means "redaction
would change nothing", and one more sanitise pass IS the proof. It unifies
the channel_scope_gate, mcp-org-isolation, and principal isolation
surfaces under one kernel.

subject: feat(legal-provenance): real-case certification — Donoghue v Stevenson [1932] UKHL 100 (#3828)
what: Added real-case certification — Donoghue v Stevenson [1932] UKHL 100 (#3828).
why:
Certifies the deterministic citation floor on genuine law, not fiction:
clean spec (4 real passages) must be SAFE TO FILE exit 0; poisoned spec
(same + 3 planted controls: inserted word, phantom holding, phantom
authority) must be DO NOT FILE with exactly 3 NOT-FOUND exit 1. Pins the
tolerance boundary (whitespace passes, one changed word fails) and fold
idempotence (re-verification is a byte-identical no-op). Judgment text
excerpted verbatim from the uni-trier.de PDF mirror of the BAILII version
(full-text sha256 e704212059bd9dd2..., OCR artefacts preserved); full
provenance chain in the example README. First run live on the CC+ team
call 2026-07-03 (receipts IDR sha256:1c443e0ba / sha256:984a5d8bd).

subject: feat(relay): gateway sentinel key so claude -p has non-empty auth (#3824 companion) (#3829)

subject: fix: AGORA misleading-green check + grip-channel /health JID leak (#3805, #3806) (#3832)
what: Fixed aGORA misleading-green check + grip-channel /health JID leak (#3805, #3806) (#3832).
why:
The AGORA GitHub Actions job IS the check-run; GitHub derives its
conclusion from the process exit code. main() in lib/agora_pr_review.py
returned 0 unconditionally, so a FAILED review (0/3 speakers, no verdict
token) OR a genuine REQUEST_CHANGES still rendered a green checkmark --
the "misleading green" that lets a real blocker hide behind
AGORA-Green=Merge. Observed on #3800 and #3801.

subject: fix(redaction): dogfood the shield everywhere + remediate live public-chain PII leak (#3820) (#3833)
what: Fixed dogfood the shield everywhere + remediate live public-chain PII leak (#3820) (#3833).
why:
config/redaction-denylist.json was committed as git-crypt ciphertext but was
NEVER listed in .gitattributes, so git never ran the smudge filter to decrypt
it on checkout. The shield read raw ciphertext, denylist_state() returned
"ciphertext", and the shield FAILED OPEN -- the emitter published unredacted
text. This is the root cause of the live PII on the public decision chain.

subject: fix(decision-chain): self-cleaning publish — remediate the ledger before push (#3834)
what: Fixed self-cleaning publish — remediate the ledger before push (#3834).
why:
CONCURRENT-RACE GUARD. Observed live 2026-07-03: seconds after a clean
publish, a concurrent session republished a PRE-remediation ledger, re-leaking
operator/contact PII onto the public chain. Root cause: live_publish rendered
the ledger AS-IS, and the leak gate (subjects_with_leaks) only fully catches
PII when the shield is usable AND current — a session with a stale ledger or an
older denylist slips past.

subject: fix(grip-chain-sync): remediate before publish — stop the cron re-leak (#3835)
what: Fixed remediate before publish — stop the cron re-leak (#3835).
why:
ROOT CAUSE of the 2026-07-03 recurring public-chain PII leak. grip-chain-sync
(the 15-min cron path, NOT live_publish) had NO remediation: it cp-pushed the
ledger with only the narrow path/key check-leak. On a host with a git-crypt
LOCKED denylist (e.g. the VPS before the .gitattributes wiring fix), the shield
fails open -> backfill emits unredacted -> a dirty ledger publishes operator/
contact PII on every cron tick. The public repo history shows "GRIP Chain Sync"
committing DIRTY every :00/:15/:30/:45 for hours; the only clean commits were
manual re-publishes.

subject: feat(legal-receipt): L2 external-council leg — catch real quotes used for claims they refute (#3838)
what: Added l2 external-council leg — catch real quotes used for claims they refute (#3838).
why:
The floor proves a citation's words are present. It cannot prove they SUPPORT
the claim — a citation can be 100% verbatim-real and still be cited for a
proposition it does not bear, or the exact opposite. This adds the moat's second
leg to the legal receipt: build_legal_receipt(council=True) sends each
(claim, quote) pair to the L2 external-model panel (prove_it_council) and records
per-citation support verdicts plus a plain-language attorney-review advisory.

what: Added openCode model failover ladder for inbound turns (#3842).
why:

subject: feat(grasp): gog-backed Google syscall shim + grasp-bot CLI (#3843)
what: Added gog-backed Google syscall shim + grasp-bot CLI (#3843).
why:
Read-only-by-default Gmail access for GRIP. lib/grasp_google.py routes
every Google operation through one argv chokepoint that always injects
--json --readonly --gmail-no-send --no-input --account <email>, so a
send or mutate is excluded by construction, not call-site discipline.
Every result is a replayable receipt (account, message_id, thread_id,
date, from, subject, snippet). brief() summarises via the HAL syscall
and fail-softs to a deterministic digest; receipts identical either way.
bin/grasp-bot is the thin CLI (accounts/auth/search/thread/attach/brief).
25 hermetic tests incl. a Goodhart safety canary (required flags
hardcoded in the test, send subcommand forbidden) and pre-auth
config-stage detection (gog schema exit 10) mapped to a plain two-step
remediation. Broly review: PASS, 0 blockers.

the per-sender tier lock lives in the OPENCODE_CONFIG permission deny-set
injected per spawn, not in plugins (grip-safety.js is fail-open and never
the tier floor). VPS opencode 1.17.11 supports --pure (verified live).
Mutation-verified test guards the flag.

subject: fix(guard): scope the receipt + add L1 injection/dangerous-instruction floor (#256) (#3862)
what: Fixed scope the receipt + add L1 injection/dangerous-instruction floor (#256) (#3862).
why:
Council finding (grounded broly mesh, 6/6): POST /guard {"text":"rm -rf ~/"}
returned a SIGNED ALLOW. That is mechanically correct for the two checks that
ran, but (a) the receipt was UNSCOPED (no `checks` field), so it
cryptographically attested "safe" for a danger class it never checked, and
(b) the framing over-claimed ("is this text safe to hand a model?"). Fix = both.

subject: fix(security): bump undici to 6.27.0 in grip-discord-voice (4 Dependabot alerts) (#3864)
what: Fixed bump undici to 6.27.0 in grip-discord-voice (4 Dependabot alerts) (#3864).
why:
undici (transitive via discord.js) resolved at 6.24.1 - vulnerable to 4 advisories
(1 high, 1 medium, 2 low): Set-Cookie header injection, WebSocket DoS, response
queue poisoning, SameSite downgrade. Fix: caret-bounded overrides {undici: ^6.27.0}
+ lockfile regen. Same major (6.x), no breaking change. npm audit: 0 vulns.
Tests: 13/13. First dogfood of the security-healer agent (051a854c).

subject: feat(security): enable security-healer auto-heal + harden unattended trigger
what: Added enable security-healer auto-heal + harden unattended trigger.
why:
Flip GRIP_SECURITY_HEAL_AUTODISPATCH=1 in the launchd plist (remediation path
dogfood-proven via undici PR #3864). Harden bin/grip-security-heal for headless
launchd: prepend Homebrew/local bin to PATH so gh/claude/npm resolve, plus a
keychain auth fallback (grip-anthropic-external) for the dispatched claude -p when
no subscription creds present - no secret embedded in the plist. Still opens
AGORA-gated PRs only; never auto-merges.

subject: fix(anchor): native OpenTimestamps anchoring via the reference ots client (#3865)
what: Fixed native OpenTimestamps anchoring via the reference ots client (#3865).
why:
lib/crypto_anchor.py _submit_ots_digest POSTed {"hash": hex} JSON to
<url>/digest on *.pool.* hostnames that no longer resolve. The OTS calendar
protocol wants the raw digest (not JSON) and those hosts are dead, so
anchor_dual never actually anchored — only placeholder receipts existed. An
overnight run mis-reported this as 'OTS/Alchemy unavailable' when the real
calendars were up the whole time; this is the root cause of that false alarm.

subject: feat(anchor): real Bitcoin (OpenTimestamps) anchor of the decision-chain root (#3863)
what: Added real Bitcoin (OpenTimestamps) anchor of the decision-chain root (#3863).
why:
First REAL anchor of GRIP's public decision chain. Canonical RFC-6962 Merkle
root over all 501 rows of data/idr-public.jsonl (leaf = each exact JSONL line)
= 0bba0792bf9f71caa815d0c42daf27f45baf07ef9749523be666f103a7f1bcb4. The
context-fold dogfood seal (#3848) sits at leaf index 494 and its inclusion
proof verifies against the root (9 hashes). Submitted via the reference
OpenTimestamps client to the live Bitcoin calendars (PendingAttestation from
bob.btc.calendar.opentimestamps.org + finney.calendar.eternitywall.com);
upgrades to a full Bitcoin block proof in ~hours via 'ots upgrade'.

subject: fix(grip): pin 1M model variant on launch so sessions never boot 200K (#3866)
what: Fixed pin 1M model variant on launch so sessions never boot 200K (#3866).
why:
What this does (plain English): GRIP sessions sometimes started without a
1M context window, which made Claude Code's autocompact thrash (the context
refilled to the limit within a few turns, repeatedly) and forced the manual
workaround of /model -> Opus 4.8 (1M context). This makes every GRIP launch
use the 1M model for real, every time.

subject: fix(memory): resolve HOLISTIC substrate by real home slug, not a hardcoded --claude glob (#3869)
what: Fixed resolve HOLISTIC substrate by real home slug, not a hardcoded --claude glob (#3869).
why:
default_substrate() globbed 'projects/*--claude/memory'. On a ~/.grip install
(this machine runs ~/.claude -> ~/.grip via symlink) that glob matched another
operator's '-Users-[PERSON-8]--claude' project and read its stale memory
instead of this machine's '-Users-void--grip'. The warm-start kernel, the
--verify completeness ledger, and the mesh broadcast digest were therefore all
synthesised from the wrong operator's substrate.

subject: feat(rules): two-pool LLM routing — offload councils/reviews to HAL Pool B to spare the weekly limit (#3870)
what: Added two-pool LLM routing — offload councils/reviews to HAL Pool B to spare the weekly limit (#3870).
why:
Pool A (Anthropic harness: interactive REPL + claude -p + CC Task/Agent/Workflow
subagents) is weekly-limited and IS the /usage %. Pool B (HAL grip_infer -> RAILLM
-> groq/gemini/deepseek) is separate spend, ~$0, already ~94% sovereign per the
grip-llm-usage signed chain (VERIFIED, 35 calls: groq 57 / gemini 31 / xai 6 /
anthropic-cch 6% = 2 stale calls). The reclaimable weekly spend is Pool A's 63%
subagent driver — a decision-layer default (sessions reach for the Agent-tool
broly at opus/max, or claude -p), NOT a missing primitive: grip-council
--roster open, grip-sovereign-council, lib/broly_council.py and grip-opencode run
already exist and are proven sovereign.

subject: fix(prove-it): L2 council evaluates quotes in-situ rather than isolated (#3871)
what: Fixed l2 council evaluates quotes in-situ rather than isolated (#3871).
why:
Implements in-situ context windows for the L2 /prove-it council. Instead
of passing just the isolated verbatim quote to the panel, which is vulnerable
to cherry-picking, it extracts the quote's paragraph ±1 paragraph from the source document
and passes the entire window. Monotonicity holds: L1 still uses the exact
quote for the deterministic hit/not-found floor.

subject: fix(agora): funded + exogenous speakers lead the roster so AGORA survives a dead provider (#3872)
what: Fixed funded + exogenous speakers lead the roster so AGORA survives a dead provider (#3872).
why:
AGORA went 0/3 (red on PR #3870) because all three speakers failed at once:
Anthropic HTTP 400 (API credit empty), DeepSeek HTTP 402 (Insufficient Balance),
Groq HTTP 403 (Cloudflare blocks GitHub Actions runner IPs). The operator topped
up DeepSeek; this change makes the presence-gated fallback ladder put the funded
+ CI-reachable + exogenous speakers FIRST — DeepSeek, OpenAI, Qwen (none share
the Gemini judge family) — so each of the 3 roles maps to a funded speaker at
depth 0 instead of wasting attempts on dead/blocked providers.

subject: fix(agora): wire the funded DeepSeek+Qwen+xAI panel into CI (workflow env + Grok rung) (#3873)
what: Fixed wire the funded DeepSeek+Qwen+xAI panel into CI (workflow env + Grok rung) (#3873).
why:
Follow-up to #3872. That PR reordered the roster funded-first, but CI still ran
only DeepSeek (2/3, red) because the AGORA speaker set is gated by which provider
keys are injected into the workflow env — not by the roster order — and the env
carried only the dead ANTHROPIC + GROQ keys alongside DEEPSEEK.

subject: feat(ci): grip-ci-substrate composite action — run any GRIP tool in CI with zero claude-code (#3874)
what: Added grip-ci-substrate composite action — run any GRIP tool in CI with zero claude-code (#3874).
why:
Reusable GitHub composite action that packages the substrate-wiring AGORA already
proves: checkout the HAL repo for the `hal` CLI, set up Python, put HAL on PATH,
and run a GRIP command — with ZERO Claude Code CLI and ZERO Anthropic harness. Any
repo adds a GRIP-substrate CI gate with one `uses:` line + provider-key secrets.

subject: fix(ci): grip-ci-substrate flagship — isolated jobs + submodules:false (live non-Anthropic AI review in CI) (#3875)
what: Fixed grip-ci-substrate flagship — isolated jobs + submodules:false (live non-Anthropic AI review in CI) (#3875).
why:
Follow-up to #3874. The flagship "non-Anthropic AI council review in CI" step
exited 2 — and it was NOT a credential issue (an earlier claim mis-diagnosed it;
verify-canonical from the run log corrected it). The real cause: the composite
action ran twice in one job, and the SECOND actions/checkout of HAL into the
existing .hal tripped a broken 'happi-site' gitlink in HAL
(`fatal: no submodule mapping found in .gitmodules`). grip-council never ran.

subject: feat(skills): Generalize grip-first-thinking for multi-harness compatibility (#3876)
what: Added generalize grip-first-thinking for multi-harness compatibility (#3876).
why:
Replaces hardcoded constraints related to Claude Code / KONO with more generalized rules
applying to any compliant AI harness (like Hermes or OpenCode). Explicitly adds
guidance on preserving cryptographic provenance via HAPPI.md (cite.verify) without
breaking the GRIP-first context escalation hierarchy.

subject: fix(skill-dispatch): CWD-independent resolution + robust frontmatter parsing (#3877)
what: Fixed cWD-independent resolution + robust frontmatter parsing (#3877).
why:
scripts/skill-dispatch.sh resolved skills via a relative path (skills/<name>/
SKILL.md), so any harness invoking it from a directory other than the repo root
(an OpenCode or Aider session in a cloned project) failed to find the skill. It
now resolves $GRIP_DIR/skills/<name>/SKILL.md, honoring $GRIP_DIR if set, else
deriving the repo root from the script's own location — so any harness can call
it from any working directory.

subject: fix(sovereign-council): resolve provider keys CI-side (env), not Mac-keychain-only (#3878)
what: Fixed resolve provider keys CI-side (env), not Mac-keychain-only (#3878).
why:
Pure-stdlib sovereign council + demo read provider keys ONLY from the macOS Keychain (security find-generic-password, check=True), so they hard-fail on Linux/CI where no Keychain exists — the reason the flagship live non-Anthropic council review in CI could not go green. Extract lib/provider_secret.py: one env -> GRIP_

_API_KEY -> Keychain(darwin) -> Linux /proc ladder (pattern already proven in hal_council_dispatch._token and grip-overnight-wave._opencode_env; N>=3 so YSH extract). Wire into bin/grip-sovereign-council and bin/grip-sovereign-demo so ONE committed tool runs unchanged on Mac dev AND Linux CI, reading the provider env vars the CI job already sets. Keys never printed; every seam injectable. 13 Goodhart-resistant resolver tests plus the 9 existing sovereign-council tests pass.

subject: feat(sentient): red-team-hardened application v2 + L1/L2 monotonicity anchors + one-pager (#3885)

why:
Red-team vector #2 ('fuzzy matching reintroduces opinion') answered with

demand (seal f60d2ff698f55da2): (1) an L1 red cannot be rescued by a
unanimously-approving L2 panel — reds never even reach L2

why:

subject: fix(unlock): Ed25519 asymmetric signing — cross-machine unlock without a shared secret (#3890)
what: Fixed ed25519 asymmetric signing — cross-machine unlock without a shared secret (#3890).
why:
Root cause (proven): grip-unlock signed with a per-machine HMAC key, so a
code minted on the creator's machine returned bad_signature on the
requester's machine — their auto-generated keys differ, and the symmetric
scheme has no shared/public key. Confirmed by verifying a minted code on the
creator machine: it returns machine_mismatch (signature valid here), so the
cross-machine bad_signature is purely the key difference.

subject: feat(grip-channel): custom emoji generation — MT19937 + Gemini Imagen 3 (#1743)
what: Added custom emoji generation — MT19937 + Gemini Imagen 3 (#1743).
why:
Two emoji creation modes:
- /emoji <seed>: deterministic procedural patterns via Mersenne Twister
  (MT19937). Same seed = same image. Patterns: geometric, barcode,
  hashgrid, radial. 5 colour palettes. Zero API cost, instant.
- /imagine <prompt>: AI-generated images via Gemini Imagen 3 API.
  Resized to 128x128 and uploaded as custom Discord emoji.

what: Fixed sync missed turns across callLLM tiers.
why:

subject: fix(whatsapp): remove @lid catch-all in isSelfChat that misidentified all contacts (#2009)
what: Fixed remove @lid catch-all in isSelfChat that misidentified all contacts (#2009).
why:
Root cause of the "Still thinking" spam loop: isSelfChat() line 269 had a
catch-all that treated ANY @lid JID as self-chat. WhatsApp now uses @lid
format for ALL contacts, not just self-chat. When V>> sent a message to
Mia's chat, the echo (fromMe=true, jid=mia@lid) was misidentified as
self-chat, bypassed the fromMe guard, and routed through handleSelfMessage
using V>>'s allowlist entry. The responder sent "Still thinking..." to
Mia's JID, which echoed back and retriggered the same path.

subject: fix(sync): replace merge fallback with force-with-lease + skip-ci filter
what: Fixed replace merge fallback with force-with-lease + skip-ci filter.
why:
The merge-based divergence handler created self-perpetuating commit divergence:
merge commits on [ORG-1]/main prevented fast-forward, causing every subsequent
sync to take the merge path. Replace with --force-with-lease which atomically
resets [ORG-1]/main to upstream SHA while refusing to overwrite concurrent pushes.

subject: feat(meet-absorb): scaffold domain-wide delegation for org-wide Gemini Notes absorb
what: Added scaffold domain-wide delegation for org-wide Gemini Notes absorb.
why:
Add DWD infrastructure so GRIP can absorb Google Meet Gemini Notes from
any codetonight.co.za Workspace user's Drive via a service account with
subject impersonation, removing the per-user OAuth blocker.

subject: feat(meet-absorb-dwd): expand SCOPES to full Workspace suite + dynamic user enumeration
what: Added expand SCOPES to full Workspace suite + dynamic user enumeration.
why:
- SCOPES widened from 3 read-only to 34 full-access scopes covering Drive, Docs,
  Sheets, Slides, Forms, Calendar, Gmail, Admin Directory, Admin Reports, Chat,
  Tasks, Keep, Meet, People, Contacts, and Classroom. Avoids repeated
  admin.google.com re-delegation when adding new absorb targets.
- list_domain_users() added: queries Admin SDK Directory API via service
  account impersonation of a Workspace admin, paginates with cursor token,
  filters suspended/archived users, returns sorted active emails.
- _iter_all_user_pages() extracted for BIG-O clarity (single linear scan at
  the public API boundary).
- scripts/meet-absorb-dwd.py --auto now uses list_domain_users dynamically
  instead of reading a static config/dwd-users.json file. --admin-user flag
  added to override the impersonation target.
- docs/DWD-MEET-ABSORB.md updated: ALL SCOPES table, dynamic enumeration
  section, rationale for full-suite scope provisioning.
- Tests: 4 new tests bring total to 30 passing (was 26). Covers _is_active_user
  filtering, list_domain_users impersonation invariant, multi-page pagination
  concat, and SCOPES tuple completeness.

subject: fix(meet-absorb): harden gog response parsing and add multi-account precedence
what: Fixed harden gog response parsing and add multi-account precedence.
why:
- Null-coerce Google API responses: dict.get(key, []) returns None when the
  key exists with a null value (Gmail does this on empty results). Replaced
  three sites with dict.get(key) or [].
- Account selection precedence added: caller env > machine-local override
  file (awk-parsed, no sourcing to avoid pulling unrelated secrets into the
  script env) > first authed account with drive scope.
- Breaks silent fallback when multiple gog tokens exist. Adding a second
  authed account previously caused gog to refuse missing-account on every
  invocation. Script now auto-selects or reads override without manual
  flag plumbing.

subject: Merge branch 'feat/dwd-meet-absorb' into main
what: Merge branch 'feat/dwd-meet-absorb' into main.
why:
Domain-wide delegation infrastructure for org-wide Meet Gemini Notes absorb.
Service account impersonation via ALL SCOPES (34 scopes covering Drive, Docs,
Gmail, Calendar, Admin SDK, Chat, Meet, etc), dynamic user enumeration via
Admin SDK Directory API, cross-org gate, diff-tail watch mode.

subject: feat(hooks): pre-check gate — PARAMOUNT prompt pre-dispatch prefilter
what: Added pre-check gate — PARAMOUNT prompt pre-dispatch prefilter.
why:
Scans outgoing subagent/task/continuation/web-fetch prompts for linguistic
patterns that commonly trigger false-positive content refusals from upstream
model providers. Defensive security reviews, code audits, and meta-reasoning
tasks get blocked when phrased with first-person investigative framing even
when the intent is defensive. This gate catches the framing before dispatch
and returns a deny decision with rewrite hints.

subject: fix+feat: stash guard byte-equivalence + [ORG-1] mirror on pull [H031 H032 H033] (#2173)
what: fix+feat: stash guard byte-equivalence + [ORG-1] mirror on pull [H031 H032 H033] (#2173).
why:
Five files inside lib/gates/ used absolute `from gates.X import ...`
statements that only resolved when `~/.claude/lib` was on sys.path
(the hook invocation context). When the package was imported via its
canonical Python path `lib.gates.X` — for example from grip_save.py's
context-gate summary helper or from any Python module that rooted its
sys.path at `~/.claude` — the absolute imports raised
`ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'gates'`, cascading up through
`lib/gates/__init__.py:38` and breaking the entire gates package load.

the currently installable version.

subject: fix(quality): exempt __future__ imports from YAGNI unused-import check (#2200)
what: Fixed exempt __future__ imports from YAGNI unused-import check (#2200).
why:
PEP 236/563 __future__ imports are compile-time directives that change
how the Python parser treats subsequent code (e.g. `from __future__
import annotations` enables string-deferred type hints). They are never
runtime names that a body scan can verify as "used".

subject: feat(bin): grip-gemini LiteLLM proxy wrapper for Gemini 2.5 Flash fallback
what: Added grip-gemini LiteLLM proxy wrapper for Gemini 2.5 Flash fallback.
why:
Rate-limit workaround that backs Claude Code with gemini/gemini-2.5-flash
via a local LiteLLM proxy when the CodeTonight Anthropic subscription is
throttled. grip-gemini starts the proxy on 127.0.0.1:4000, waits for the
readiness endpoint, then launches `claude` (or any target) with
ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL pointed at it. PID-file lifecycle plus signal trap so
the proxy dies cleanly when the target exits.

subject: docs: grip-gemini operator guide for LiteLLM proxy workaround
what: Documented grip-gemini operator guide for LiteLLM proxy workaround.
why:
Companion reference for bin/grip-gemini (4bcf51c2). Covers macOS Keychain
setup, day-to-day usage, Windows two-terminal path, model mapping,
limitations while proxied (no extended thinking / caching / precise
token counts), smoke-test commands, and a troubleshooting table.

subject: feat(grip-gemini): optimal exponential backoff for Gemini 2.5 Flash rate limits (#2290)
what: Added optimal exponential backoff for Gemini 2.5 Flash rate limits (#2290).
why:
Gemini 2.5 Flash on the free tier caps at ~10 RPM with a 60s reset
window. The old LiteLLM config set num_retries: 2, which gives ~3s of
jittered backoff — well below the reset window. Users hit 429s in
Claude Code whenever a burst exceeded the per-minute quota.

subject: docs(grip-gemini): correct re-seed instruction — status does not bootstrap
what: Documented correct re-seed instruction — status does not bootstrap.
why:
The migration snippet in docs/litellm-proxy.md incorrectly claimed that
grip-gemini status re-seeds the default config template. It does not —
cmd_status only reads state and runs a health probe. Only cmd_run and
cmd_proxy_foreground call require_config(), which is the function that
writes the heredoc template to config/litellm-config.yaml when absent.

subject: docs(vps): add grip-remote VPS bootstrap runbook
what: Documented grip-remote VPS bootstrap runbook.
why:
Consolidates the rebuild procedure for grip-remote (Hetzner CAX) into
one reference. Covers both the snapshot-restore migration path (for
upgrading to a bigger plan in a different datacenter when Rescale is
out of stock) and the fresh Ubuntu 24.04 bootstrap path (for clean-slate
rebuilds or architecture changes).

subject: fix(context-gate): use API usage counts, eliminating 17x token undercount
what: Fixed use API usage counts, eliminating 17x token undercount.
why:
Problem: the chars/4 heuristic in estimate_tokens_from_jsonl() summed only
message.content char lengths. For a live session the API reported 508k
tokens while the gate saw 30k — a 17x undercount that silently disabled
the 70/80/85% soft checkpoints across every session on Opus 4.7 with the
1M context window.

subject: fix(140.85): real message count, live fitness, freshest generation + delight fields
what: Fixed real message count, live fitness, freshest generation + delight fields.
why:
Three data bugs in bin/codec-call:
- Messages showed "?" because read_instance() returned top-level index.json
  (which has no message_count). Now pulls from instances[-1].message_count.
- Fitness showed 0.000 because genome.json has neither "fitness" nor
  "composite_fitness" keys. New fallback chain walks through fitness_history[-1]
  before defaulting to 0.0. Live value is 0.364.
- Generation showed stale top-level value (430). Now uses
  instances[-1].lineage.generation (482) as the authoritative source.

subject: fix(context-gate): derive tokens_used from pct in snapshot
what: Fixed derive tokens_used from pct in snapshot.
why:
get_context_utilisation_snapshot() was returning raw estimated_tokens
as tokens_used. Same bug as the statusline's total_input_tokens: a raw
token count independent of the percentage, causing minor inconsistency.
Now tokens_used = int(pct * budget / 100) — always derived from pct,
matching the statusline fix principle.

subject: fix(context): align save-exit and gate with CC native context percentage
what: Fixed align save-exit and gate with CC native context percentage.
why:
Statusline now writes CC's authoritative context_window.used_percentage to
state/features/cc-native-context.json after each render. The context gate
and get_context_utilisation_snapshot() read this sidecar first (max_age=120s
for gate, 300s for display), falling back to JSONL scan only when no fresh
native data exists.

subject: feat(context-gate): per-provider LitM thresholds + post-compact sidecar reset
what: Added per-provider LitM thresholds + post-compact sidecar reset.
why:
- context_gate.py: add HAL mismatch guard to _read_cc_native_context(); when
  the sidecar's context_max differs from CONTEXT_MAX by >2x (HAL sub-1M
  provider vs native 1M), return None and fall back to JSONL estimate

subject: fix(hooks): remove unused time import from post-compact-hook
what: Fixed remove unused time import from post-compact-hook.
why: YAGNI: import time was never referenced in this module.

subject: fix(hal): cap max_tokens for DeepSeek and Ollama; fix WeasyPrint render path
what: Fixed cap max_tokens for DeepSeek and Ollama; fix WeasyPrint render path.
why:
DeepSeek (cloud and Ollama-hosted) enforces max_tokens <= 8192. Claude Code
sends the Claude API default (~16k+), causing a 400 BadRequest. Fix applied
at the LiteLLM proxy layer via litellm_params.max_tokens: 8192 on all
DeepSeek and Ollama deployments.

why:

subject: fix(routing): word-boundary guards on pattern phrases prevent substring false positives

subject: fix(whatsapp): suppress self-reply loop via sent-message-ID cache
what: Fixed suppress self-reply loop via sent-message-ID cache.
why:
Text messages echoed back from the WhatsApp server were re-processed
by the upsert handler and re-emitted as incoming messages, triggering
an infinite reply loop. The fix tracks IDs of outgoing messages via a
per-session Set (30s TTL), and the upsert handler skips any message
whose ID was registered. Unlike a blanket fromMe skip, this only
suppresses echoes of messages sent by this session — messages from
other linked devices are unaffected.

subject: feat(skills): /self-healing-ci — recursive nightly regression healer
what: Added /self-healing-ci — recursive nightly regression healer.
why:
New skill installs a self-healing CI loop into any GitHub project: nightly
GH Action runs the full test suite; each regression spawns an inner converge
loop that MUST produce a fix AND a new regression-anchor test that fails on
pre-fix code. MESH council reviews the batch; no auto-merge. Non-gamable
criterion means the test suite grows monotonically with every bug caught.

subject: fix(kono): restore semantic recall + ETH framework absorption
what: Fixed restore semantic recall + ETH framework absorption.
why:
- lib/kono_substrate.py: _execute_hybrid_search was calling
  engine.search_hybrid (no underscore) but EmbeddingEngine only
  exposes _search_hybrid privately. AttributeError silently swallowed
  by bare except, returning [] for every recall query. Symptom: 0
  results despite 2128 embeddings. Fix: import search_hybrid +
  apply_recency_decay from embeddings.search submodule directly,
  per the documented "use submodules directly for ISP" pattern.
  Verified: recall "[ORG-1] API staging" returns 3 hits (was 0).

attempts, no log noise from the lib/guild_semantic_analyzer.py pipeline
hitting sqlite_lembed_compat ModuleNotFoundError.

subject: feat: per-session active-llm marker + history stack (once/undo) + council stacking (#2440)
what: Added per-session active-llm marker + history stack (once/undo) + council stacking (#2440).
why:
Three coherent enhancements to lib/active_llm.py:
1. Per-session marker isolation — wires _state_file() through set_active /
   set_council / clear_active / show_active. Each function accepts an optional
   cc_pid kwarg. When set (or when GRIP_CC_PID env var present), the marker
   lands at \${TMPDIR}/grip-session-active-llm-\${cc_pid}.json instead of the
   shared GLOBAL_STATE_FILE. Statusline resolves the same path so different
   sessions see their own active LLM. Closes task #1423.
2. Marker history stack — every set_active / set_council captures the prior
   marker as previous, capped at depth 5 via _truncate_stack. Enables undo
   and once-revert without ad-hoc state.
3. One-shot + undo — set_active_once writes a mode=solo-once marker with
   revert_after_consumes=1; consume_once_marker is the HAL-side hook that
   reverts after consumption. undo_active pops one entry off the stack.
   CLI: /llm once <model>, /llm undo, /llm stack.

subject: fix(worktree-reaper): squash-aware merge predicate + flatten estimate_disk_mb (#2450)
what: Fixed squash-aware merge predicate + flatten estimate_disk_mb (#2450).
why:
Problem observed live this session: 11 of 13 stale worktrees were
incorrectly classified KEEP because git's merge-base/--merged predicate
returns false for squash-merged branches (the original branch tip is
not an ancestor of the new squashed commit on main). GRIP's admin path
uses --squash exclusively, so this false-negative blocks reaping for
every PR shipped via /sprint, /auto, or admin-merge.

power-user gets `isolation/<user>-personal`, protected by ruleset 15803953,

requires the `leak-check` job to pass before merging into main. To make
this safe for ALL PRs (not just isolation/* ones), the workflow's
job-level `if:` was replaced with a step-level gate that emits green
status for non-isolation PRs and only enforces denylist matching for
isolation/<user>-personal and feat/promote-* sources.

subject: fix(kono): bypass dedup for session summaries (H299)
what: Fixed bypass dedup for session summaries (H299).
why:
The store_session_memory dedup gate (0.7 word-overlap, 4h window)
false-positive matched session summaries because the canonical format
"Gen N | INSTANCE | M msgs | ACHIEVEMENT" has only 5-7 tokens — with
"Gen", "msgs", and "|" repeated across every session, two distinct
sessions hit ~75% overlap. KONO silently skipped every summary; the
surface error "embedding pipeline unavailable" was misleading (the
pipeline worked fine; dedup was rejecting the input).

needs -r explicitly).

distinguishes V>>'s direct Anthropic API key from any CCH (Claude Code
Harness) subscription credentials.

costing ~80-200ms per call. This sidecar runs once via --serve, reads JSON
lines from stdin, writes JSON line responses on stdout, dropping per-call
cost to <10ms after first warm-up.

subject: feat(fold-search): novel DFI-FIB fixed-point file search algorithm + donna script removal
what: Added novel DFI-FIB fixed-point file search algorithm + donna script removal.
why:
Fold-Search (lib/fold_search.py): novel search algorithm applying the
fold-the-kernel Banach fixed-point property to transitive file discovery.
Terminates when ℱ(found) = found — same idempotency guarantee as the OCF
compactor. Uses DFI-FIB Fibonacci depth schedule for natural acceleration.

subject: feat(banach-projector): N-dimensional Banach fixed-point coordinator-convergence kernel
what: Added n-dimensional Banach fixed-point coordinator-convergence kernel.
why:
Generalizes fold_search (1D Banach over breadth) and converge (1D iterative
over depth) into a coupled N-dimensional Banach projector. State is an
N-tuple; N operators each project the state onto one component. Joint fixed
point P(S) = S reached when every operator is idempotent within epsilon.

until an inbound message triggered lazy cleanup.

subject: fix(grip): cmd_fresh preserves CWD when invoked from project dir (closes #2637) (#2652)
what: Fixed cmd_fresh preserves CWD when invoked from project dir (closes #2637) (#2652).
why:
Per [PERSON-6]'s report (issue #2637, 2026-05-07): gg++ launched from a real
project directory (e.g. ~/repos/[ORG-1]-voice-switch) silently jumped to
~/.claude because cmd_fresh in bin/grip unconditionally cd'd into
$CLAUDE_DIR. The operator's project CWD was lost; subsequent !pwd, file
lookups, worktree creates, and git operations all defaulted to ~/.claude
instead of the project. Regression introduced by 7ceaea7a (2026-04-28).

subject: fix(agora): refuse to render hallucinated reviews — fail loud (PARAMOUNT) (#2634)
what: Fixed refuse to render hallucinated reviews — fail loud (PARAMOUNT) (#2634).
why:
Origin: 2026-05-06. PR #2633 received 2 hallucinated AGORA reviews back-to-back
(Speaker B produced a "config/redis.go" review for a Python-only PR). Root
cause: diff truncation hit a 4000-char window of HTML/JS bytes from a broken
favicon file before reaching real code, so Speaker B got webserver-adjacent
garbage and fell back to a generic Go/Redis review template.

subject: feat(nugrip): safe one-command GRIP upstream upgrade (#2633)
what: Added safe one-command GRIP upstream upgrade (#2633).
why:
VSCode spawns gg++-vscode without inheriting the shell env that defines
GRIP_USE_GEMINI, so 'set -euo pipefail' aborted line 52 before exec.
Use ${GRIP_USE_GEMINI:-false} so the wrapper falls through to the real
claude binary when the variable is unset.

subject: feat(mesh): auto-register + inbox-poll + live-dump hooks for cross-session context transfer
what: Added auto-register + inbox-poll + live-dump hooks for cross-session context transfer.
why:
Wires grip-session-mesh into every Claude Code session via three PostToolUse
hooks. Before this change the mesh server was running but no session called
session_register — the presence DB sat empty/stale and concurrent sessions
could not address each other through mesh tools. After this change every
session auto-joins on its first tool call, surfaces inbound mesh messages as
visible signals, and publishes a refreshable live-state snapshot.

subject: feat(mesh): bin/mesh KISS CLI for 1-to-many + many-to-1 + many-to-many context transfer
what: Added bin/mesh KISS CLI for 1-to-many + many-to-1 + many-to-many context transfer.
why:
Adds a 167-line bash dispatcher at bin/mesh and updates skills/mesh/SKILL.md
to document the wired surface. Builds directly on the three PostToolUse hooks
shipped in 97403849 — every concurrent CC session already auto-registers,
poll-reads inbox, and refreshes a LIVE.md snapshot, so this CLI just exposes
the three topologies as one-shot commands.

subject: fix(relay): rename relay-dispatcher.ecosystem.cjs -> .config.cjs for pm2 auto-detect (#2692)
what: Fixed rename relay-dispatcher.ecosystem.cjs -> .config.cjs for pm2 auto-detect (#2692).
why:
pm2 only treats *.config.{js,cjs} / ecosystem.config.* as ecosystem
files; a plain *.ecosystem.cjs is run as a script (bogus entry, no
process) — the GRIP #2678 cutover hit exactly that and had to start the
dispatcher via explicit `pm2 start lib/relay_dispatcher.py --interpreter
python3`. This rename makes `pm2 start config/relay-dispatcher.config.cjs`
work as intended. Deploy comment corrected to the real surgical-checkout
path. No code/behaviour change. Follows #2690 / closes the #1798 fast-follow.

what: Added concurrent-session commit-guard for shared git-crypt tree.
why:
Systemic mechanical layer for the recurring friction class (51 insight +
26 hypothesis telemetry matches, 2026-04-25 to 2026-05-15): under >=2

subject: fix(grip): only inject dev-channel flag for LOCAL grip-channel MCP (#2678)
what: Fixed only inject dev-channel flag for LOCAL grip-channel MCP (#2678).
why:
Post-W10 grip-channel is a remote {"type":"http"} entry (the VPS
relay-dispatcher). _grip_channel_registered() only checked key presence,
so bin/grip injected --dangerously-load-development-channels
server:grip-channel for a server Claude Code cannot load locally
("server:grip-channel not found" at startup) AND turned every `grip`
launch into a competing VPS-daemon inbound subscriber (the 2026-05-13
#[PROJECT-1]-chats race shape). Now gates injection on command-present AND
type!=http; emits a clear VPS-relay note otherwise. Outbound
channel_reply unaffected. Syntax-checked; guard verified against the
live .mcp.json (SKIP — correct).

subject: feat: legal-tech AI news ingest service (webhook -> dedup -> rank -> KONO -> digest) (#2697)
what: Added legal-tech AI news ingest service (webhook -> dedup -> rank -> KONO -> digest) (#2697).
why:
Phase 1 of the LinkedIn legal-tech monitoring stack converged via
research-mode (KWatch.io keyword-net + multifollow.io account-watch +
changedetection.io open-web, all SaaS-side; account-ban risk offloaded
per the Proxycurl lesson). Sibling of relay_dispatcher.py but an
ingest->dedup->digest pipe, not a message->reply relay: stdlib HTTP,
SHA-256 FIFO-capped dedup, word-boundary relevance scorer, canonical
absorb syscall, batched Slack digest via the slack_transport shim
(token never at the call site -> guardrail-compliant by construction).

subject: fix(hypothesis): coerce numeric fields in from_dict (#1826) (#2729)
what: Fixed coerce numeric fields in from_dict (#1826) (#2729).
why:
`verify --all` crashed with "unsupported operand type(s) for -:
'float' and 'str'": Hypothesis.from_dict passed raw JSON straight into
the dataclass with no coercion, and Python dataclasses do not enforce
annotations at runtime. hypotheses.jsonl is edited out-of-band and
carries legacy entries, so baseline/actual_value/effect_size arrive as
strings ("0.5", "") or null. A str baseline detonated at the first
arithmetic (effect_size, line 219) and latently in the
"increase"/"decrease" path of _evaluate_prediction and the verify
CLI's :.3f format.

subject: feat(rules): default every session to /caveman for token economy (V>>) (#2732)
what: Added default every session to /caveman for token economy (V>>) (#2732).
why:
V>> directive: always enable /caveman to save tokens. Implemented as an
always-loaded rule (rules/*.md load into every session's context at
start) rather than a settings.json SessionStart hook — those are
regenerated by install.sh --update and would not persist. This mechanism
is durable, version-controlled, zero extra per-session exec, and ships
safely via PR (no live PreToolUse blast across concurrent sessions).

subject: fix(rtk): pass through rg/git-grep in command position (#1804) (#2728)
what: Fixed pass through rg/git-grep in command position (#1804) (#2728).
why:
RTK 0.38.0 inspects only the leading token, so `cd <worktree> && rg ...`
(the dominant pattern once the shell cwd resets each Bash call) fell
through to `rtk hook claude`, which rewrote rg->`rtk grep` (breaking
ripgrep-only flags) and DENIED any command containing `grep` -
including `git grep`, a distinct index-aware tool ripgrep cannot
replace. That is the reproduced "rg broken / grep guardrail" deadlock.

subject: fix(grip): preserve CWD for nascent project launches (#1880) (#2793)
what: Fixed preserve CWD for nascent project launches (#1880) (#2793).
why: cmd_fresh gated cd-to-CLAUDE_DIR behind _has_project_marker (#2637). A brand-new project dir has no markers yet, so the create-a-new-repo workflow was misclassified as a relay launch and silently abandoned to ~/.claude (operator believes session is in the project; relative-path ops hit the GRIP repo; /compact surfaces it via !pwd). Tighten the gate: cd only when markerless AND $PWD == $HOME (the genuine relay signature). Export GRIP_LAUNCH_DIR before any cd as a durable source for the SessionStart hint (OLDPWD is clobbered by Claude Code's re-exec). Harden project-cwd-hint.sh to read GRIP_LAUNCH_DIR and never false-hint $HOME. #2637 relay behaviour preserved (all marker tests green); 4 Goodhart-proof guard tests execute the real extracted guard.

subject: fix(grip-pty-repaint): wall-clock deadline ends interactive REPL freeze (#2886)
what: Fixed wall-clock deadline ends interactive REPL freeze (#2886).
why:
The first-frame box-erase buffered ALL child output until the box regex
matched or 8192 bytes accumulated. Claude Code v2.1.143's box does not
match _CC_BOX_RE; CC prints a sub-8192-byte box+prompt then idles for
input, so neither exit fired and the live TUI was withheld forever
(keystrokes echoed into the withheld buffer, dumped only on 8KB
overflow). The file's 'fail-safe by construction' invariant was false
for the interactive case.

subject: fix(grip_generation): generation is install-global, not cwd-keyed (#2888)
what: Fixed generation is install-global, not cwd-keyed (#2888).
why:
resolve_generation keyed the generation off the launch directory's
projects/<enc(cwd)>/grip/index.json. Launching GRIP in ~/.hal (no
per-folder lineage index) reported 'new project (no GRIP history here)'
on the SAME startup screen where KONO recalled that folder's history —
a visible self-contradiction. GRIP's generation is one continuous RSI
lineage of the *installation* (~/.claude), not a property of the cwd:
running in ~/.hal is still the same GRIP at the same Gen N.

subject: fix(statusline): mirror _resolve_cc_pid key priority — close #2931 reader/writer mismatch
what: Fixed mirror _resolve_cc_pid key priority — close #2931 reader/writer mismatch.
why:
#2932/#2943 wired the /llm CLI per-session writer (CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID
first). statusline-command.sh::row2_model still keyed _cc_pid on
GRIP_CC_PID->ppid only, OMITTING CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -> reader/writer
keyed differently -> per-session marker never found -> silent fallback to
clobbered global -> wrong model shown (the residual #2931 symptom + the
exact path-contract mismatch #2931's falsification clause predicted).
Fix: derive _cc_pid as CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID->GRIP_CC_PID->ppid, byte-
mirroring lib.active_llm._resolve_cc_pid priority (unify the contract).

subject: test(reasoning-audit): Goodhart-proof coverage for reasoning_audit.py (#2823)
what: Added tests for goodhart-proof coverage for reasoning_audit.py (#2823).
why:
Rule-14 coverage gap: lib/reasoning_audit.py is the /catch-me-up audit
backend — integrity-sensitive (classifies, falsifies and persists
session claims; a silent bug mislabels a Disproved claim Verified). Per
llm-augmentation-pattern this layer has NO LLM augmentation, so its
correctness is purely deterministic and must be mutation-proof. Shipped
on origin/main with zero tests. 29 exact-value tests, zero mocking on the
deterministic core (_safe, _extract_section, _catalogue, _classify,
_determine_verdict evidence>20 + PR/commit-source gate, _falsify,
_translate_plain bucket-order + singular/plural pivot, run_audit
coordinator); the two filesystem boundaries via real tmp files with only
STATE_DIR patched. The end-to-end test documents a deliberate SUT
property surfaced while writing it: context-sourced claims are
conservative-by-design (never auto-verify without explicit evidence or a
PR/commit source). Pattern: tests/test_holistic_backlog.py (PR #2820).

subject: fix(hal-council): per-round fallback in-use guard — close #2866 thundering-herd
what: Fixed per-round fallback in-use guard — close #2866 thundering-herd.
why:
>=2 concurrent 429'd primaries with colliding fallback chains all hammered
the shared first fallback in one council round. Added a round-scoped
thread-safe reservation (_inflight): _dispatch_one skips a fallback already
being substituted-into concurrently → next in chain. Correctness preserved
— _dedupe_active still dedupes by responding pid; an exhausted chain still
honestly degrades to quorum_failure (#2725). Reservation per-round,
released in finally (sequential reuse still allowed).

subject: fix(grip-anywhere): exact datetime stale-PR boundary (AGORA #3018 review)
what: Fixed exact datetime stale-PR boundary (AGORA #3018 review).
why:
Converge delta on AGORA review concerns:
- #1 (real, fix): _count_stale_prs uses direct aware-datetime compare
  (cutoff = now - timedelta(days=7); updated < cutoff) instead of float
  .timestamp() arithmetic — boundary now mathematically exact, no
  float-rounding/DST sub-second drift at the ~7-day cutoff.
- #4 (real, fix): boundary test delta tightened 60s → 1s; exact compare
  kills a `<`→`<=` mutation deterministically at 1-second granularity.
- #2 (out-of-#2894-scope): _gh timeout is the pre-existing graceful-
  degrade contract shared by all 3 fetchers; stale_prs:int count
  contract precludes a -1 sentinel — separate pre-existing question.
- #3 (YAGNI): #2894 asked only to *distinguish* fetch-failure from a
  genuine zero (done + tested in _workflow_count -1 vs 0); surfacing a
  tri-state to RepoHealth has no present caller — speculative.

subject: fix(install): menu/diff to stderr in interactive prompts; unblocks Tresia (#3107)
what: Fixed menu/diff to stderr in interactive prompts; unblocks Tresia (#3107).
why:
ask_installation_mode, ask_merge_strategy, ask_conflict_resolution are
called via command substitution -- mode=$(ask_installation_mode), etc.
Bash command substitution captures stdout, so menu echoes to stdout
were silently absorbed into the caller's variable, leaving the operator
with a bare 'Choice [1/2/3]:' prompt and no labelled options.

`min_fitness` parameter filters the orthogonal `fitness_score` column
at `_enrich_single_hit:1472` — so low-similarity hits pass the fitness
gate unfiltered and surface in warm-start at scores like 0.01.

lib/code_index.py was an 841-line monolith silently shadowed by the
lib/code_index/ package. Python's import machinery prefers packages of
the same name, so 'import code_index' always resolved to the package's
__init__.py — anyone editing the monolith was editing dead code, with
zero behavioural effect on the live code-index MCP server (the package).
