subject: feat(grip-starter-kit): sanitised framework packager + coexistence installer (#3169)
what: Added sanitised framework packager + coexistence installer (#3169).
why:
Package a clean, history-free copy of the GRIP framework for a
collaborator. lib/grip_starter_kit.py is the allowlist packager: it copies
only named framework paths, withholds client-specific skills, scrubs
operator/client tokens from worked examples, and runs a two-profile
leak-scan that ABORTS on any client name, secret, or operator ID found in
a framework file. scripts/install-starter.sh is the recipient-side
coexistence-aware installer -- additive merge over an existing framework
with every overwrite backed up first. skills/grip-starter-kit/SKILL.md is
the operator entry point.

subject: fix(agora): tolerate Gemini's 'Judge synthesis' header in reasoning extraction
what: Fixed tolerate Gemini's 'Judge synthesis' header in reasoning extraction.
why:
Root cause: Gemini 3.1 Pro (current judge) emitted '### 4. Judge synthesis
(why this verdict)' instead of prompt-mandated '### Reasoning'. Parser
regex required literal 'Reasoning' → no match → empty judge_reasoning →
fallback message ('no synthesis paragraph') rendered AS the synthesis.
Self-referential symptom V>> caught on PR #3211 R1 AGORA output.

subject: feat(wa): auto-broadcast session digests to principals on /save + /closeout (#1993) (#3211)
what: Added auto-broadcast session digests to principals on /save + /closeout (#1993) (#3211).
why:
V>>-ratified HITL policy (2026-05-24 post-PR-#3205 wizard):
- Trigger: /save + /closeout final step (best-effort, never blocks).
- Cadence: time-gated to 4h between broadcasts (won't spam saves).
- Recipients: V>> always; Craig only when content is DONNA-related.
- Format: short WhatsApp digest of recent GRIP/HAL/HAPPI happenings
  (trigger header + achievement + last 5 commits + open-hypothesis count).
- Off switch: GRIP_WA_PRINCIPAL_BROADCAST=off env var.
- Cooldown tuning: GRIP_WA_PRINCIPAL_BROADCAST_COOLDOWN_S env var.

per JIT triple-gate: argparse + O(1) dispatch table over public kernel

kernel-logic duplication. CC max=2 per function (target ≤5). ~65 LOC.

subject: feat(agora): add launchd plist + installer for true durable scheduling
what: Added launchd plist + installer for true durable scheduling.
why:
CronCreate in this environment turned out to be session-bound (durable=true
was accepted as a parameter but the runtime labelled the job 'session-only').
For V>>'s 'runs without fault' requirement — survives Claude restart,
machine reboot, terminal close — launchd is the macOS-native durable
scheduler.

subject: feat(scrollback): pre-idle prefix-compaction to cheapen unavoidable cache-miss reprocess
what: Added pre-idle prefix-compaction to cheapen unavoidable cache-miss reprocess.
why:
Adds lib/prefix_compaction.py (new SRP module — compaction of the live
conversation prefix before an idle gap, distinct from scrollback_lru.py's
retrieval concern). Strategy: always keep system + last K turns verbatim;
replace older messages with deterministic role-tagged head/tail digests.
No LLM call in the core path (optional injected callback). Token estimate
chars/4 heuristic. Honest framing: does NOT prevent the 5-min cache-miss;
cheapens it from ~88k to ~≤20k tokens (≥4x). H-COMPACT-1 registered
(deadline 2026-06-30).

subject: feat(oss): prompt-cache-keepalive — keep LLM prompt cache warm across idle gaps
what: Added prompt-cache-keepalive — keep LLM prompt cache warm across idle gaps.
why:
Provider-agnostic keepalive (injected touch_fn, zero SDK deps) that solves the
~5-min prompt-cache TTL: touch the cached prefix before eviction so the next
turn is a cache-read, not a full reprocess. Includes a falsifiable cost model
(net_savings, breakeven_resume_probability) proving the win for short-idle
resumes and bounding the honest-loss regime (capped touches). 17 CBC tests,
network-free (injected touch_fn + sleep).

That is the single most user-FELT surface in GRIP and it violated Rule 17
(plain language) + canonical-output-style rail #1.

subject: feat(usm): mid-session self-refresh closes the staleness gap (#3281)
what: Added mid-session self-refresh closes the staleness gap (#3281).
why:
What this does (plain English):
When you start a GRIP session, the inherited memory already tells GRIP the
exact next step, so plain "continue" works without /recall. That next step is
fresh at session START. But during a LONG session (an overnight loop runs for
hundreds of messages), the next step keeps evolving while the block GRIP first
read stays frozen at boot-time. Nothing re-shows GRIP its own freshly-updated
next step mid-flight. This adds that missing piece: a tiny, rate-limited check
that re-surfaces the current next step when it changes during a session.

subject: feat(hal): GRIP shim wiring prompt-cache-keepalive to HAL's Anthropic cache path
what: Added gRIP shim wiring prompt-cache-keepalive to HAL's Anthropic cache path.
why:
Thin syscall-doctrine adapter (build_cache_touch / keep_hal_cache_warm) so a
GRIP autonomous loop can keep a HAL Anthropic prompt cache warm across an idle

transport + apply_cache_control; uses the pip-installed prompt-cache-keepalive
package. 5 network-free CBC tests (importorskip-guarded for envs without the pkg).

What this does (plain English): GRIP counts its own assets (skills, agents,
commands, rules, hooks, modes) in more than one place, and those places had

another. This makes lib/asset_counter.py the single place that does the
counting and points both callers at it, so the numbers can never silently
disagree again.

what: Documented wire README inventory counts to the canonical counter (#3290).
why:
README's Agents/Commands reference lines hardcoded stale counts (41 agents,
84 commands) that contradicted the freshly regenerated docs/AGENTS.grip (38)
and docs/COMMANDS.grip (88). Add per-category grip-stats markers so those two

subject: fix(context-gate): never HALT a dispatched sub-agent (a dispatch-incomplete root cause) (#3297)
what: Fixed never HALT a dispatched sub-agent (a dispatch-incomplete root cause) (#3297).
why: A dispatched sub-agent's PreToolUse hook inherits the parent session's context % (sub-agents share the parent session_id, so the context gate reads the parent's per-session sidecar). Under a deep parent, every sub-agent tool call hit the >=85% interactive HALT branch and was denied at iteration 0 — a deadlock, since a sub-agent has no operator to answer a HALT and cannot /compact itself. This was a hidden mechanical cause of the dispatch-incomplete symptom that no prompt-engineering fix in agent-dispatch-protocol.md can reach.

subject: feat(grip-channel): WhatsApp reconnect governor v1 (ban-resilience core) (#3296)
what: Added whatsApp reconnect governor v1 (ban-resilience core) (#3296).
why:
Circuit-breaker + full-jitter backoff + token-bucket reconnect limiter +
DisconnectReason routing, with cross-restart state persistence so a fresh
process resumes the cooldown (ban-canary) instead of resetting it. Addresses
the 2026-05-29 WhatsApp anti-abuse block caused by reconnect churn (repeated
daemon restarts). 21 Goodhart-proof tests. Pure module only — live-daemon
wiring is a separate reviewed follow-up.

subject: fix(agora): single-file PR grounding false-positive (port of HAL #388) (#3299)
what: Fixed single-file PR grounding false-positive (port of HAL #388) (#3299).
why:
Ports HAL's #388/#389 fix to GRIP's agora_pr_review.py. L3 diff-grounding
required EVERY graded speaker to re-type a diff file path; on a single-file PR
a fluent prose Speaker B legitimately omits it, so the council wrongly rendered
REVIEW INVALID. Grounding is now a COUNCIL property on single-file PRs: flag
only when NO non-filler speaker cites the one file. Multi-file per-speaker
defense unchanged. Extracted _real_diff_files (DRY; centralises the PR #3140
root-file dedup) + _grounding_reasons.

subject: feat(image): channel-agnostic /imagine + /emoji on WhatsApp/Slack/Web (H791 W2 W4) (#3291)
what: Added channel-agnostic /imagine + /emoji on WhatsApp/Slack/Web (H791 W2 W4) (#3291).
why:
Wires the merged image-gen kernel + imagineDeliver pipeline (PR #3288) into
the WhatsApp, Slack, and Web inbound paths so /imagine and /emoji fire on
every channel — not just Discord.

subject: test(commander-security): correct $() assertion — sanitizeRiscInput never stripped parens (#3300)
what: Added tests for correct $() assertion — sanitizeRiscInput never stripped parens (#3300).
why: The 'strips $() command substitution' test asserted ( and ) are stripped, but sanitizeRiscInput (commander-security.ts) strips only the 6 active shell-meta chars (backtick, $, backslash, NUL, CR, LF). SHELL_META_CHARS has NEVER contained parens (introduced 455f7afb 2026-05-04, never changed). Stripping $ already neutralises $() substitution (no $ -> no substitution), and the RISC input reaches Python via a stdin-piped spawn (no shell) so bare () is inert; stripping parens would also mangle legitimate messages like '(see attached)'. This was a test over-assertion, NOT a security regression — it failed silently because the grip-channel vitest suite is not a pull_request CI gate (runs only on push-to-main via deploy-grip-channel.yml).

subject: feat(grip-channel): wire Reconnect Governor into live WhatsApp daemon (#3301)
what: Added wire Reconnect Governor into live WhatsApp daemon (#3301).
why:
Closes the WhatsApp reconnect control loop. Every connect attempt now passes
through the Governor (circuit breaker + token bucket + full-jitter backoff) and
every connection result feeds back via onSuccess/onFailure. State persists across
restarts so a restart storm can no longer bypass the guards — the cross-restart
ban-canary. The unbounded scheduleReconnect loop (30s cap, no breaker, reset on
every restart) is replaced by governed cadence.

subject: fix(grip-channel): rebuild on ANY changed source, not just index.ts (#3303)
what: Fixed rebuild on ANY changed source, not just index.ts (#3303).
why:
run-daemon.sh's staleness check only compared src/index.ts mtime against
dist/index.js, so a change to any other source (e.g. whatsapp.ts) silently
shipped stale: on restart the daemon rebuilt nothing and ran old compiled code.
Surfaced 2026-05-29 when the Reconnect Governor wiring (#3301) nearly deployed
as a no-op — the restart skipped the rebuild because index.ts was unchanged.

subject: fix(grip-channel): pass WhatsApp web version to makeWASocket for stable connection
what: Fixed pass WhatsApp web version to makeWASocket for stable connection.
why:
The WhatsApp bridge built its socket without specifying a version, so Baileys
fell back to its bundled default. WhatsApp closes that connection with a bare
"code 0 / status 0" stream end almost immediately. The reconnect governor
counts each drop as a failure, trips the circuit breaker, and then silently
holds every reconnect — so the bridge never shows as connected even when the
linked-device credentials in .wa-session are perfectly valid. This presented
as a phantom "WhatsApp won't connect" with no useful error.

subject: feat(cache): dogfood proof — prompt-cache-keepalive keeps a real Anthropic prefix warm (#3310)
what: Added dogfood proof — prompt-cache-keepalive keeps a real Anthropic prefix warm (#3310).
why:
Live A/B against the real Anthropic API (grip-anthropic-external) proving the
oss/prompt-cache-keepalive technology: two distinct ~16k-token cached prefixes idle
420s (> the ~300s TTL). The untouched baseline RE-WROTE the whole prefix on resume
(cache_creation=16028, cache_read=0); the keepalive arm — one real PromptCacheKeepalive
touch at t=270s via grip_cache_keepalive.build_cache_touch — resumed as a warm read
(cache_read=16026, cache_creation=3). Net ~16.8k token-equiv saved on one 7-min idle;
breakeven resume-probability 0.087.

subject: fix(hooks): wire time-awareness hook into canonical settings reference (#3311)
what: Fixed wire time-awareness hook into canonical settings reference (#3311).
why: The time-awareness UserPromptSubmit hook (closes #2568) was built and unit-tested but never added to config/settings.reference.json. It lived only in gitignored local settings.json, silently disconnected about 11 days ago, and GRIP lost real wall-clock awareness with nothing guarding the wiring. Wire it (blocking, to guarantee the time-context injection reaches the model) and add a regression anchor that fails if the hook is dropped from the reference again, or if any wired UserPromptSubmit hook points to a missing file.

subject: docs(research): OpenClaw transport resilience syscall-doctrine study (#3307)
what: Documented openClaw transport resilience syscall-doctrine study (#3307).
why:
Study of how OpenClaw (375k-star OSS, Baileys-based, shared ex-CIPS/ClawdBot
ancestry with GRIP) keeps chat transports connected, mapped onto GRIP's
grip-channel daemon. Patterns learned from merged OpenClaw PRs and
re-expressed in GRIP's architecture — no source copied (OpenClaw is
NOASSERTION).

subject: docs(research): ClawHub compatibility design for GRIP/HAL (#3309)
what: Documented clawHub compatibility design for GRIP/HAL (#3309).
why:
Design for how GRIP/HAL becomes ClawHub-compatible (OpenClaw's public skill
registry). ClawHub skills are versioned text bundles built on SKILL.md — the
same primitive GRIP already uses — so compatibility is a transport-and-trust
problem, not a format-translation one.

subject: Harden WhatsApp transport resilience (408 pairing retry, queue drain, dead-socket guard) (#3306)
what: Harden WhatsApp transport resilience (408 pairing retry, queue drain, dead-socket guard) (#3306).
why:
Adapt four merged OpenClaw Baileys-resilience patterns (re-implemented in
GRIP's own style, not copied) to make the WhatsApp bridge survive the
failure modes that previously broke pairing and dropped sends:

subject: feat(clawhub): read-only digest-verified ClawHub reference resolver (#3312)
what: Added read-only digest-verified ClawHub reference resolver (#3312).
why:
Layer 1 of GRIP's ClawHub compatibility (designed in
docs/research/clawhub-compat-design.md, merged separately): resolve a
clawhub:<pkg>[@<version|tag>] reference against the public ClawHub HTTP API,
verify the artifact content digest, surface the registry trust verdict, and
return normalised metadata. Pure information retrieval — the least dangerous
thing GRIP can do with a third-party registry.

subject: fix(grip-channel): align 8 stale tests with canonical behaviour across 4 suites (#3314)
what: Fixed align 8 stale tests with canonical behaviour across 4 suites (#3314).
why:
What this does (plain English): four test suites in mcp-servers/grip-channel
had 8 failures that show up even on a clean copy of main. In every case the
test was checking for an OLD behaviour, while the real code had deliberately
moved on to a newer, documented behaviour. This change updates the stale tests
(and two out-of-date code comments) to match what the code actually does now,
so the suite is green again. No production code behaviour changes -- only tests
and docstrings.

subject: feat(grip-notify): multi-channel fallover ladder (#3313)
what: Added multi-channel fallover ladder (#3313).
why:
grip_notify can now route a notification through an ordered ladder of
transport shims (Slack / WhatsApp / email / macOS) with two modes:
first-success (stop at the first reachable channel — the resilient
default) and fan-out (try all). A down channel (WhatsApp is offline now)
is recorded as a failed delivery and the ladder continues to the next
rung — it can never block the others.

subject: feat(grip-hear): structured --json hearing (#3308)
what: Added structured --json hearing (#3308).
why:
Adds `grip-hear --json <file>` — returns a machine-readable hearing
{genre, mood, language, instrumental, vocals, key_lyric, energy, darkness,
one_line} instead of prose. energy and darkness are 0.0-1.0 axes grounded in
the audio (the two axes of an emotional-field map). Fail-safe parser: clamps
the axes to [0,1], tolerates code fences, fills missing keys, and raises on
prose-only output. 8 Goodhart-proof tests.

subject: docs(rules): port discipline + reserved-port registry (PARAMOUNT) (#3316)
what: Documented port discipline + reserved-port registry (PARAMOUNT) (#3316).
why: A dev-server preview broke: launch.json assigned grip-web port 5173 (Vite's framework default) while apps/grip-web/vite.config.ts pins 3000, and vite bound IPv6 only so the IPv4 probe also refused. Adds rules/port-registry.md (the reserved-port registry + read-the-actual-port discipline + previews-must-match-port-and-bind-all-interfaces), plus a Port Discipline block in CLAUDE.md, the rules index row, and an AGENTS.md cross-tool note, so the discipline loads every session. References operational-context.md for the VPS table rather than duplicating it (DRY).

subject: chore: snapshot working tree — catalogue tooling, grip-hear, gate test, state churn

subject: fix(agora): deterministically refute false "missing import" PR-review blockers (#3318)
what: Fixed deterministically refute false "missing import" PR-review blockers (#3318).
why:
AGORA speakers and the Gemini judge reason from the truncated `gh pr diff`
hunk, so an import on an unchanged line is invisible — a symbol used in a
changed line looks unimported. PR #3315 cost ~10 review cycles chasing a
phantom "gen / count_inventory / inspect referenced with no import ->
NameError on load" blocker when all three were imported (lines 17/25/27 of
the full file). Confirmed recurrence of the same diff-truncation FP class
recorded in feedback_agora_judge_advisory_ignore_false_positives.

subject: docs: keep README inventory counts fresh + add a CI guard (#3315)
what: Documented keep README inventory counts fresh + add a CI guard (#3315).
why:
Follow-up to PR #3290 (which wired the README agent/command counts to
`lib/asset_counter.count_inventory()` via `<!-- grip-stats:KEY -->` markers)
and PR #3293 (which reconciled the skills line to skills/INDEX.md).

subject: docs(research): add Pattern F (#50091 proxy/version-refresh) + broader PR survey (#3321)
what: Documented pattern F (#50091 proxy/version-refresh) + broader PR survey (#3321).
why:
The original study covered five patterns from five OpenClaw PRs. This
update adds:

subject: docs(research): add SKILL.md frontmatter spec + rate-limit handling note (#3322)
what: Documented sKILL.md frontmatter spec + rate-limit handling note (#3322).
why:
The base compat-design document established the three-layer architecture
(resolve / consume / publish) and the HAPPI envelope model. This update adds
two concrete details needed to implement Layer 2 (consume):

subject: feat(clawhub): resolver with 429 rate-limit handling + Goodhart-resistant tests (#3323)
what: Added resolver with 429 rate-limit handling + Goodhart-resistant tests (#3323).
why:
lib/clawhub_resolver.py — Layer 1 ClawHub resolver with digest verification,
trust verdict gate, and 429 rate-limit handling (retry_after_s surfaced to
caller; trust 429 → blocked fail-safe). _urlopen_fetch extracts HTTPError
headers before reading body so Retry-After survives a body-read failure.

subject: fix(agora): give the thinking-model judge enough token budget (ELI5 no longer truncates) (#3324)
what: Fixed give the thinking-model judge enough token budget (ELI5 no longer truncates) (#3324).
why:
The AGORA judge (gemini-3.1-pro-preview) is a thinking model: its thinking
tokens count against the Gemini maxOutputTokens budget, sharing the pool with
the visible response. The judge inherited _build_envelope's shared default of
2048, which is fatally tight — thinking on a 3-speaker synthesis is large and
variable, and when it nears 2048 it crowds out the visible output. The result
was the truncated ELI5 seen in review comments ("...bake identical cookies
every time. Right", with the Reasoning/synthesis section never generated) and,
when thinking spiked further, an empty response surfacing as "Judge
unavailable". That budget contention is the real source of the judge's
non-deterministic verdicts on identical code.

subject: docs: OpenClaw WhatsApp transport study (syscall-doctrine analysis) (#3327)
what: Documented openClaw WhatsApp transport study (syscall-doctrine analysis) (#3327).
why:
Analyses five merged OpenClaw PRs (#88183, #73732, #72145, #79083, #50091)
plus six additional transport/reconnect PRs, drawing out patterns that inform
GRIP's WhatsApp transport hardening roadmap.

subject: docs(memory): D0 cross-project memory digest in global CLAUDE.md (#3325)
what: Documented d0 cross-project memory digest in global CLAUDE.md (#3325).
why:
What this does (plain English): GRIP, HAL and DONNA each keep their own
memory in a separate folder. Until now nothing told a session that those
sibling stores exist or where the shared rules live. This adds a short
"Cross-project memory (D0)" section to the always-loaded global CLAUDE.md
so every session, in any project, knows: (1) where the D0 root index is,
(2) that cross-cutting RULES live in rules/*.md and fold "up", and (3) the
three project memory silos and which working directory each maps to.

subject: docs(rules): AGORA-green = merge + pragmatism stuck-loop admin-merge (#3320)
what: Documented aGORA-green = merge + pragmatism stuck-loop admin-merge (#3320).
why:
Encode V>>'s standing policy: when the AGORA Council verdict is PASS/MERGE
with 0 confirmed blockers and no REQUEST_CHANGES/DEFER_CHANGES, that
cross-provider review IS the exogenous ratification — merge without asking
the operator. Asking "merge once AGORA is green, or hold it for you?" blocks
progress and is redundant.

subject: docs: rename reference docs from .grip to plain Markdown (.md) (#3326)
what: Documented rename reference docs from .grip to plain Markdown (.md) (#3326).
why:
The three reference docs used a bespoke `.grip` extension and DSL that no
renderer understood and no editor highlighted -- it only existed to feed a few
regex parsers, and it predates the GRIP name (CIPS era). Not [ORG-1]-specific
(no .grip anywhere under organizations/[ORG-1]). This renames all three to
standard Markdown so they render on GitHub and are greppable/editable.

subject: docs: cross-reference transport study; mark Layer 1 implemented; add transport-doctrine note (#3328)
what: Documented cross-reference transport study; mark Layer 1 implemented; add transport-doctrine note (#3328).
why:
- Add companion-document link to docs/research/openclaw-transport-study.md
  (the Wave 1 WhatsApp transport study that grounds the resilience doctrine
  this design builds on)
- §6 delivery table: mark Layer 1 (lib/clawhub_resolver.py + tests) as
  Implemented and cross-link the test file
- New §4.1 Transport-doctrine note: the five OpenClaw transport invariants

  reconnect-governor discipline; fail-safe matches the watchdog-reset pattern

subject: tests: add edge-case coverage for clawhub_resolver (#3329)
what: add edge-case coverage for clawhub_resolver (#3329).
why: Six new Goodhart-resistant tests extending the existing suite:

subject: fix(agora): defensive setdefault for judge max_tokens flag override (#3330)
what: Fixed defensive setdefault for judge max_tokens flag override (#3330).
why:
_dispatch_judge set envelope["flags"]["max_tokens"] unconditionally,
assuming _build_envelope always emits a "flags" key. It does today
(lib/hal_council_dispatch.py::_build_envelope always returns a dict with
"flags"), so there is no live crash — but the override should not depend
on that implicit invariant. Use setdefault("flags", {}) so the override
stays correct if _build_envelope's emitted shape ever changes.

subject: fix(docs-gen): parse dotted/namespaced skill IDs in generate-skills-index (#3331)
what: Fixed parse dotted/namespaced skill IDs in generate-skills-index (#3331).
why:
The list-item regex `[A-Za-z0-9][\w-]*` did not include `.` or `:`,
so `legal-ops.formal` parsed to `legal-ops` and
`plugin:skill`-style IDs would be similarly truncated.

subject: fix(agora): deterministic judge verdicts — temperature 0.0 (+ setdefault guard) (#3332)
what: Fixed deterministic judge verdicts — temperature 0.0 (+ setdefault guard) (#3332).
why:
Completes the AGORA judge reliability work. PR #3324 gave the thinking-model
judge enough token budget to finish its answer; this makes the verdict
deterministic. The judge envelope now sets temperature 0.0 so the SAME diff
yields the SAME verdict run-to-run — the original failure (PR #3315 saw
REQUEST_CHANGES x8 / DEFER x1 / Judge-unavailable on identical code) was the
combination of truncation (fixed in #3324) AND sampling variance (fixed here).

subject: fix(embeddings): expose public store_embedding/get_stats/search_hybrid; fix skill_indexer tests (#3333)
what: Fixed expose public store_embedding/get_stats/search_hybrid; fix skill_indexer tests (#3333).
why:
skill_indexer -- and plan_registry, learning_detector, session_control_server,
embeddings/cli.py -- call engine.store_embedding(), .get_stats(), .search_hybrid(),
but the base EmbeddingEngine only defined private _store_embedding/_search_hybrid
and no get_stats at all, so every such call raised AttributeError once the code
path was reached. Add the public methods (delegating to the storage/search module
functions), keeping the historical private names as aliases for existing callers.

subject: fix(embeddings): restore get_engine GRIP_EMBED_BACKEND dispatch + db_path (#3334)
what: Fixed restore get_engine GRIP_EMBED_BACKEND dispatch + db_path (#3334).
why:
The registry-shim refactor (443df9686) replaced get_engine's body with a
bare `return get_engine_for()`, dropping the W6 env-var backend dispatch and
the db_path parameter. test_embedding_dispatch.py was never updated, so its
6 tests went red: "get_engine() got an unexpected keyword argument 'db_path'".

subject: fix(professional-pdf): full-bleed @page background (failure class 6)
what: Fixed full-bleed @page background (failure class 6).
why:
A non-white page colour set only on html/body paints just the content box in
WeasyPrint — the @page margins stay white, so it renders as an inset panel with
white gutters, not full-bleed. Added `background` to the @page rule in the
hardened CSS template (reference.md) and a 6th failure class + cadence bump to
the audit (SKILL.md). Bug-prevention memory gets Rule 11 (machine-local).

subject: fix(grip-channel): start-path-agnostic daemon env defaults — stop pm2 fatal-loop
what: Fixed start-path-agnostic daemon env defaults — stop pm2 fatal-loop.
why:
The Mac grip-channel daemon vanished from the pm2 list ("Process or Namespace
grip-channel not found") after a bare `pm2 restart`. Root cause: verify-env.sh
hard-required GRIP_CHANNEL_TRANSPORT + GRIP_CHANNEL_MCP_PORT, but on Mac those
two come ONLY from pm2's ecosystem.config.cjs env block. Any start path that
bypasses `pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs` (bare restart, `pm2 start
run-daemon.sh`, launchd, stale-dump resurrect) left them unset, so verify-env.sh
exit-1'd ten times past max_restarts and the app was dropped from the list.

subject: feat(relay): operator-gated full-capability sessions on the relay dispatcher (#3338)
what: Added operator-gated full-capability sessions on the relay dispatcher (#3338).
why:
Lets V>>'s own Slack/WhatsApp inbound drive a full-capability claude -p
session (no --tools lock) so a relayed session "can do anything", while
every non-operator sender keeps the Read,Grep,Glob read-only lock unchanged.

subject: fix(grip-channel): re-fetch WA web version on reconnect (code-0 self-heal) (#3336)
what: Fixed re-fetch WA web version on reconnect (code-0 self-heal) (#3336).
why:
The WA web version was fetched once in connect() and cached in _waModules; every
reconnect reused the cache. A transient boot-time fetch failure left version=
undefined for the life of the process, so the reconnect path applied the bundled
Baileys default — which WhatsApp closes with code 0 / status 0 on every attempt,
tripping the reconnect governor's breaker into a permanent flap recoverable only
by a full process restart.

subject: fix(linkedin-feed): capture RSC pagination + Voyager per-post API (#3337)
what: Fixed capture RSC pagination + Voyager per-post API (#3337).
why:
LinkedIn obfuscated the feed DOM (UUID componentkeys, hashed class names, zero
semantic anchors) — the old CSS selectors matched 0 posts on a live logged-in
feed. Rewire the extract layer: intercept LinkedIn's RSC (React Server
Components) pagination stream via CDP Fetch.enable, pull activity URNs from the
wire body, then call the stable Voyager per-post API for structured content.
The old CSS-selector path is kept as an ordered fallback.

subject: feat(relay): enable operator full-capability on the VPS relay (#3338) (#3339)
what: Added enable operator full-capability on the VPS relay (#3338) (#3339).
why:
V>>-authorised 2026-05-31: set GRIP_RELAY_OPERATOR_FULL_CAPABILITY=1 in the
relay-dispatcher pm2 env so the operator's own Slack/WhatsApp inbound drives
a full-tool-surface session; every other sender stays Read,Grep,Glob locked.

subject: feat(relay): per-message context modes — /fresh starts a new session (D2) (#3340)
what: Added per-message context modes — /fresh starts a new session (D2) (#3340).
why:
Operator per-message context control on the relay (V>>'s '1,2,3'):
- mode 2 (warm): default — resume the per-channel session (unchanged)
- mode 3 (fresh): leading /fresh or /new starts a NEW session, ignoring the
  warm one; keyword stripped so claude never sees it
Mode 1 (resume operator's live terminal session) follows with the Mac->VPS
transcript sync (next wave).

subject: feat(grip-hear): librosa deterministic tempo/key mechanical floor (#3341)
what: Added librosa deterministic tempo/key mechanical floor (#3341).
why:
What this does (plain English): grip-hear currently lets the language model
guess a track's tempo (BPM) and musical key, which it can hallucinate. This
adds a mechanical "floor" that measures those two facts from the audio signal
itself with librosa, so they are never made up. The model still describes how
a track feels; tempo and key now come from maths.

subject: feat(grip-hear): MCP hear tool — listen to a track mid-conversation (#3342)
what: Added mCP hear tool — listen to a track mid-conversation (#3342).
why:
Add a `hear` MCP tool so GRIP can listen to a local audio track
mid-conversation. Input is an audio file path (plus optional model/prompt
overrides); output is the structured reading from scripts/grip-hear.py
(genre, mood, energy, darkness, language, vocals, key lyric) merged with the
deterministic librosa tempo/key floor from lib/grip_hear_floor.py, plus
provenance fields.

subject: feat(grip-hear): Gemini Files API for oversize tracks (#3343)
what: Added gemini Files API for oversize tracks (#3343).
why:
What this does (plain English): grip-hear lets GRIP "listen" to music by
sending an audio file to Google's Gemini model. Until now the audio was
always embedded directly inside the request (base64). Gemini caps that
inline path at ~20MB, so longer tracks — full arrangements, long phone
recordings — simply failed.

subject: feat(grip-hear): Afrikaans lyric layer via Lelapa Vulavula (#3344)
what: Added afrikaans lyric layer via Lelapa Vulavula (#3344).
why:
Give grip-hear a REAL lyric layer for Afrikaans (and other Lelapa-supported
languages) via Lelapa.ai Vulavula STT, so a vocal track's reading carries
actual transcribed lyrics with provenance rather than only the model's guess.

subject: feat(grip-hear): Whisper fallback for non-Vulavula lyric languages (#3345)
what: Added whisper fallback for non-Vulavula lyric languages (#3345).
why:
What this does (plain English): grip-hear's lyric layer transcribes a song's
words via Lelapa Vulavula, which only covers a fixed set of (mostly South
African) languages. For a vocal track in a language Vulavula does not cover —
or when Vulavula is reachable but returns nothing — there was no real
transcription, only the model's guessed line. This adds an optional Whisper
fallback so those tracks still get real lyrics, tagged so provenance stays
honest.

subject: fix(relay): /fresh|/new recognised behind a leading @-mention (real Slack flow) (#3346)
what: Fixed /fresh|/new recognised behind a leading @-mention (real Slack flow) (#3346).
why:
The relay receives RAW inbound text, so '@GRIP /fresh X' arrives as
'<@U…> /fresh X' — _parse_context_mode anchored the keyword at index 0, so
/fresh was silently treated as warm and the literal '/fresh' reached claude.
Confirmed by code-trace: slack.ts handleInbound relays raw.text;
bridge-utils.postRelayWebhook forwards message.content unstripped.

subject: feat(grip-catalogue): display floor (tempo/key) + lyrics, fix lyric-layer no-op (#3347)
what: Added display floor (tempo/key) + lyrics, fix lyric-layer no-op (#3347).
why:
The deterministic librosa floor (tempo/key/mode) and the Vulavula lyric
layer were emitted by grip-hear but never surfaced: build_catalogue.py
dropped the floor fields when assembling each record, and the site
template never rendered them. This wires both through end to end.

subject: feat(grip-catalogue): render English translation block for Afrikaans lyrics (#3350)
what: Added render English translation block for Afrikaans lyrics (#3350).
why:
The lyric layer (grip_hear_lyrics.transcribe_lyrics, fixed in #3347) and the
Vulavula afr->eng translate path are both verified live: Koningin transcribes
to 1038 chars of Afrikaans and translates to 669 chars of English. The site
rendered the transcribed lyrics but had no place for the translation.

subject: fix(grip-channel): honour .grip-channel.env on macOS + gitignore it (#3348)
what: Fixed honour .grip-channel.env on macOS + gitignore it (#3348).
why:
The macOS branch of load-env.sh loaded secrets only from Keychain and never
sourced the local .grip-channel.env, so Mac-local overrides (e.g. WA_SOCKS_PROXY)
were silently ignored — only the Linux/VPS branch sourced it. Source it (set -a,
so vars export to the node daemon) after the Keychain block. .gitignore: add
.grip-channel.env (secret-bearing local dotenv; the existing '.env' rule did not
cover it).

subject: fix(grip-channel): serialise WhatsApp connect drivers (code-0-before-QR root cause) (#3351)
what: Fixed serialise WhatsApp connect drivers (code-0-before-QR root cause) (#3351).
why:
What this does (plain English):
The WhatsApp daemon could not relink — every attempt died with a "code 0"
error before the QR code even appeared. Root cause: the daemon has THREE
things that can each start a WhatsApp connection — the initial boot, the
auto-reconnect after a drop, and a 60-second health watchdog. They were not
coordinated, so two could run at once. Each new attempt closes any existing
socket first (`if (this.sock) this.sock.end()`); when a second driver started
while the first was still ~2 seconds from showing its QR, it killed the first
one's socket mid-handshake. WhatsApp reports that as "code 0 / status 0", and
the QR never rendered.

subject: fix(grip-catalogue): strip machine-local absolute paths before embedding in public site (#3352)
what: Fixed strip machine-local absolute paths before embedding in public site (#3352).
why:
grip_catalogue_site.py embedded the full catalogue.json records into index.html,
including each record's absolute `file` path (for example
[redacted]<user>/Music/SoundCloud/X.mp3). The generated site is public, so this
leaked the operator's home directory and username into a public page, violating
the no-machine-path-leak rule. Only the relative `audio` path is needed to play.

subject: feat(grip-hear): route music through HAL provider ladder (#3349)
what: Added route music through HAL provider ladder (#3349).
why:
Route audio through HAL's /api/infer media path (HAL #414) so grip-hear can
hear music via HAL's provider ladder (failover + audit) instead of a direct
Gemini REST call.

subject: fix(grip-channel): repair pm2-inherited c-ares DNS + serve hinted lookups (WA flap) (#3353)
what: Fixed repair pm2-inherited c-ares DNS + serve hinted lookups (WA flap) (#3353).
why:
What this does (plain English)
------------------------------
After re-pairing WhatsApp, the Mac grip-channel daemon would connect, reach
"open", then drop within seconds and repeat ("code 0" flapping), so inbound
WhatsApp was unreliable. A standalone single-socket Baileys client with the
identical creds, session dir, and socket options stayed rock-stable — so the
cause was the daemon's reconnect machinery, not the credentials, WA version,
network, or number.

subject: feat(git-solve): squash-aware supersession + Banach fixed-point/idempotency proof (#3365)
what: Added squash-aware supersession + Banach fixed-point/idempotency proof (#3365).
why:
What this does (plain English): /git-solve can now tell when an AI-agent branch is
SAFE TO DELETE even after a squash-merge — the exact case GitHub's own
`git branch --merged` gets wrong. It adds a mathematical proof (a Banach
fixed-point / idempotency witness) that "merging this branch changes nothing", so
reconciliation has a provable, replayable stopping point instead of a guess.

subject: feat(task-drift): reconcile harness tasks against reality (W1-W3 + L4 gate) (#3367)
what: Added reconcile harness tasks against reality (W1-W3 + L4 gate) (#3367).
why:
Solve task-list drift (the append-only ledger consumed as a state database):
a harness task's status is self-report (telemetry), never criterion. The
criterion is the exogenous referent — a URL that returns 200, a PR that is
merged, a file that exists. extract_referents() pulls url/pr/path/repo from
task text via one priority-ordered alternation regex; probe_referent() returns
factual existence/state, DIP-injected so tests are hermetic.

subject: chore(donna): retire Cytoscape portal — archive code, keep workstreams (#3368)
what: Maintained retire Cytoscape portal — archive code, keep workstreams (#3368).

The donna.grip-web.com Cytoscape "Live Knowledge Portal" was RETIRED
(V>>-decided 2026-06-01): it served data frozen 16 days behind a green
"fresh" pill (integrity bug), its source was security-deleted, and it is
superseded by the live donnaoss.com/api/workstreams tracker. Operational
decommission (pm2 delete donna-portal, launchd unloaded) was done
separately; the Cloudflare dashboard route removal is an operator follow-up.

subject: feat(scope): cross-org/individual scope-contamination kernel — idempotent-no-op proof (#3371)
what: Added cross-org/individual scope-contamination kernel — idempotent-no-op proof (#3371).
why:
What this does (plain English):
GRIP runs across many organisations and individuals at once, so the recurring
safety question is always the same — is this content / memory / output CLEAN to
surface in a given (org, individual) place, or does it carry material that
belongs to a DIFFERENT scope? This kernel answers that question by proving that
SANITISING the content for the target scope is a no-op: if scrubbing every
out-of-scope token changes nothing (sanitised == original), the content was
already clean. That equality IS the proof — the SAME idempotent-no-op proof
git-solve's merge-tree uses, transplanted from git to a non-git domain. Because
it works by scrubbing, the exact leaked spans come out for free, like merge-tree
handing you the exact conflict hunks.

subject: fix(task-drift): extractor precision (dogfood-found) + docs (#3370)
what: Fixed extractor precision (dogfood-found) + docs (#3370).
why:
The post-merge live reconcile of the real task ledger surfaced 3 fail-safe
false-opens (false-OPEN, never false-CLOSE) — precision edges the hermetic suite
missed:
- slash noun-lists ('deploy/publish/cid/list') matched the repo pattern -> repo
  regex now rejects multi-slash chains via a complete-token trailing lookahead
  (?![\\w/-]) that defeats the backtracking hole a bare (?!/) left;
- git branch refs ('feat/donna-mcp-scaffold') matched repo, and 'scaffold' inside
  the compound false-triggered the existence verb -> branch-prefix exclusion +
  verb boundary (?<![\\w-])v(?![\\w-]) instead of \\b (no hyphen-adjacent match);
- a cross-repo path ('tests/test_notarise.py', a DONNA file) probed absent from
  the GRIP cwd falsely contradicted -> path-absent demoted to UNVERIFIABLE
  (url-down + repo-absent stay confident).

subject: feat(grip-channel): GRIP_CHANNEL_NO_WHATSAPP gate — VPS skips WA bridge (W10)
what: Added gRIP_CHANNEL_NO_WHATSAPP gate — VPS skips WA bridge (W10).
why: VPS + Mac were contending for the single WhatsApp device-link, flapping the Mac with code-0 disconnects (reconnect-governor classifies this as concurrent connect drivers). Gate: when GRIP_CHANNEL_NO_WHATSAPP=1 the daemon does not register WhatsApp in the bridges map, so it is never connected or watchdog'd; the bridge object still exists so relay/responder references stay valid. Default off — Mac unaffected. Symmetric to the Mac WA-only sentinel in load-env.sh.

subject: feat(sigil): earned proof-of-work sigils — collectible delight (/sigil) (#3380)
what: Added earned proof-of-work sigils — collectible delight (/sigil) (#3380).
why:
Novel user-facing delight: a tiny deterministic glyph-stamp minted ONLY for a
verified milestone (merged PR, CONFIRMED hypothesis, CI-green ship, converged
sprint). Generated purely from the milestone's own bytes via SHA-256, so it
cannot be forged without doing the work — the sigil IS the proof-of-work, made
beautiful. Coheres with rules/exogenous-anchor-law.md: delight rides a verified
exogenous signal, never a self-claim. Sigils accumulate in a browsable passport.

subject: fix(mesh): honest live-session count + dead-tombstone GC (no stale-framing) (#3381)
what: Fixed honest live-session count + dead-tombstone GC (no stale-framing) (#3381).
why: bin/mesh state/collect over-counted: it globbed every cc-*-LIVE.md and every undelivered inbox row as 'current work', so it reported e.g. '1342 messages waiting' when ~1333 were addressed to sessions that exited days ago. V>> flagged this as stale-framing — 'no STALE work as framing downstream'.

subject: docs(rules): lifecycle-closed-or-expired — name the stale-framing bug class (PARAMOUNT) (#3385)
what: Documented lifecycle-closed-or-expired — name the stale-framing bug class (PARAMOUNT) (#3385).
why: Generalises PR #3381's mesh stale-framing fix into a design-time rule. The bug was an instance of a class: a record born open, with no guaranteed closer, no TTL, and a report that counts the whole open set as 'current work' — so dead records accrete and get framed as live. A /ultrathink found the same shape across harness tasks, git index locks, the hypothesis engine, and telemetry JSONL.

subject: feat(cold-tier): ColdContextPager — GRIP demand-paging mechanism (Deliverable B step 1) (#3386)
what: Added coldContextPager — GRIP demand-paging mechanism (Deliverable B step 1) (#3386).
why: MECHANISM half of the cold-tier token-saver (Deliverable A = merged prompt-cache-keepalive ColdStore, PR #2). Offloads an idle/cold GRIP context slice to the zero-token lossless FileRingStore and pages it back instead of re-deriving. Three CBC gates: ECONOMICS (offload only when cold_net_vs_rederivation>0 — page-back bills ~1.25x), SCOPE (PARAMOUNT — deny_unknown raises PermissionError on client/financial/donna; never reaches disk; no-sensitive-financial-in-grip.md), LOSSLESS (exact round-trip). DIP-injected store+econ. 5 Goodhart tests (each fails if a gate is removed).

subject: fix(grip-save): soft-skip memory-brief on no-instance project (#3393)
what: Fixed soft-skip memory-brief on no-instance project (#3393).
why:
The memory-brief inject (unified_memory_brief.py --inject-memory) hard-failed
(exit 1) when the target project had no grip/index.json, so any /save run from
a not-yet-GRIP-initialised project logged a spurious "1 failed" in the tally.
The inject is best-effort: a project with no instance has nothing to inject —
a clean no-op, not a failure.

subject: feat(cold-tier): wire cold-compaction arm into the live context-gate (V>>-ratified live-gate) (#3392)
what: Added wire cold-compaction arm into the live context-gate (V>>-ratified live-gate) (#3392).
why:
Deliverable B live-gate wiring. The autonomous critical-threshold path
(_observe_critical_threshold) already allows + trusts CC native autoCompact and runs a
defensive checkpoint; this adds an OPT-IN, FAIL-OPEN cold-slice offload as part of that
checkpoint via lib/cold_compaction_gate.offload_for_autonomous_compaction, so GRIP cold
state can page back token-cheap after compaction instead of being re-derived.

subject: feat(cold-tier): step-4 token-savings dogfood — proves offload+page-back < re-derive (#3395)
what: Added step-4 token-savings dogfood — proves offload+page-back < re-derive (#3395).
why:
Deliverable B step 4 (the exogenous proof). scripts/dogfood_cold_tier_savings.py runs a REAL
merged-ColdStore round-trip over a representative GRIP cold-slice set and measures the savings
against the ColdTierEconomics model:

subject: feat(grip-tier): self-heal Max-class plans off the pro 180K floor (#3398)
what: Added self-heal Max-class plans off the pro 180K floor (#3398).
why: A SessionStart hook reads Claude Code's exported CLAUDE_CODE_SUBSCRIPTION_TYPE and, when it is Max/Team/Enterprise (Opus 1M included), persists tier=max so the NEXT launch stops exporting CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW=180000 and lets the full Opus 1M window stand. Fixes the papercut where a Max user is silently floored to pro/180K because detect_tier() cannot distinguish Max from Pro before a session exists (the '1M context label on a 180K window' symptom in docs/grip-tier-1m-context.md).

subject: fix(grip-channel): serve cached DNS for hinted lookups (ws/Agent flap) (#3397)
what: Fixed serve cached DNS for hinted lookups (ws/Agent flap) (#3397).
why:
What this does (plain English): the DNS cache that was meant to keep WhatsApp
connected when the daemon's normal name-lookup wedges was secretly doing
nothing for the connections that actually matter.

subject: feat(plain-language): explain_genome — genome fitness in plain English, always (#3399)
what: Added explain_genome — genome fitness in plain English, always (#3399).
why:
The genome 'fitness' number (e.g. 0.429) is the most-misread GRIP value — it
reads like a 43% grade but is actually the MEAN usefulness across ~401 skills
on a 0-1 scale, where most skills sit dormant near the 0.5 neutral baseline, so
~0.4-0.5 is the healthy steady-state. explain_genome() makes that legible,
losslessly (exact numbers preserved, only prose plain), per
rules/canonical-output-style.md.

subject: feat(genome): genome_insights — plain-language leaderboard + change analysis (A+B) (#3401)
what: Added genome_insights — plain-language leaderboard + change analysis (A+B) (#3401).
why:
Deliverable A: explain_leaderboard() — top-N active skills by fitness with
WHY from contribution_record; active/dormant segment (never static bottom-N
on saturated values); lossless .3f fitness, exact counts.

subject: fix(cold-tier): replace tautology dogfood with a REAL, falsifiable token measurement (#3400)
what: Fixed replace tautology dogfood with a REAL, falsifiable token measurement (#3400).
why:
The previous dogfood_cold_tier_savings.py was a TAUTOLOGY — it summed hard-coded
rederivation_tokens minus a constant restore_cost and always printed 148,125, proving nothing
(caught by V>> 2026-06-02; AGORA missed it, nitpicked imports). Removed.

subject: fix(context-gate): don't false-HALT the first tool call after /compact (#3402)
what: Fixed don't false-HALT the first tool call after /compact (#3402).
why:
The context gate (Gate 9) estimates live context via
lib/gates/context_gate.py::estimate_tokens_from_jsonl, which reverse-scans
the conversation JSONL for the last message.usage entry. After /compact the
harness shrinks the IN-MEMORY context but does NOT shrink the on-disk JSONL:
it appends a compaction-boundary entry and keeps every prior line. So on the
FIRST tool call after /compact the scan returned the PRE-compaction turn's
usage (e.g. 853,959 = ~85% of a 1M window) and the gate false-HALTed that
call on a stale estimate. It self-healed only once a fresh post-compaction
turn wrote a new low usage entry (confirmed live 2026-06-02: a Bash call
HALTed at "Estimated 853,959 tokens" immediately post-compact; the identical
call succeeded one turn later).

subject: fix(grip-yolo): canonical PYTHONPATH=root + lib.gates import (fixes ModuleNotFoundError) (#3404)
what: Fixed canonical PYTHONPATH=root + lib.gates import (fixes ModuleNotFoundError) (#3404).
why:
bin/grip-yolo's 3 heredocs used PYTHONPATH=$CLAUDE_DIR/lib + 'from gates.yolo_mode', but importing
the gates package runs lib/gates/__init__.py, which eagerly imports orchestrators that use 'from
lib.X' absolute imports (e.g. context_gate.py -> 'from lib.state_manager'). Those need the repo
ROOT on PYTHONPATH, not .../lib — so 'grip-yolo status|on|off' raised
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'lib.state_manager'. Latent on every clone (skills use the
canonical 'PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude' + 'from lib.gates...' form and bypass this CLI wrapper; a
[ORG-1] dev ran it directly and surfaced it). Aligned all 3 heredocs to the canonical convention.

what: Added teach-yourself-GRIP skill — 3 runnable lessons, observable gates, learner-invoked delight (#3409).
why:
Add /dojo: a hands-on, three-lesson tour of GRIP's core moves
(prompt-coach, save+recall, converge). A learner doesn't read about

completes once the command demonstrably ran (its own output is the

subject: fix(gates): temp-script gate matches delimited tokens, not substrings
what: Fixed temp-script gate matches delimited tokens, not substrings.
why:
check_temp_script flagged any filename whose stem CONTAINED 'temp'/'tmp'/'script'
as a substring -- false-positiving on transcript_source.py (the 'script' in
'tran*script*'), description.py, subscription.py, template.py, temporary.py. Now
splits the stem on _ - . and matches exact tokens; real temp scripts (temp_foo,
my_script, tmp, script, test_temp) still denied. Regression test in
tests/test_gate_fixes.py (test_gate7_substring_falsepos_allowed, passing).
Origin: 2026-06-02 donna-hear build.

subject: feat(ultraclear): zero-loss context partition — a fresh window with no information loss (#3410)
what: Added zero-loss context partition — a fresh window with no information loss (#3410).
why:
ULTRACLEAR is the WHAT-to-keep planner that sits above lib/cold_context_pager.py's
WHAT-to-move mechanism. Instead of /compact (which re-derives a lossy summary by
reprocessing the whole window at ~0.10x base per token), it partitions the session
three ways and offloads rather than summarises, so a fresh interactive window
carries zero information loss by construction:

what: Fixed remove dead _terse_intent refs from test_relay_ack (#3411).

subject: test(grip_save): behavioural anchor for memory_brief flag gate (#2129) (#3413)
what: Added tests for behavioural anchor for memory_brief flag gate (#2129) (#3413).
why:
AGORA #3393 deferred hardening the memory_brief flag anchor. The existing tests
are source-introspection (grep grip_save.py for the flag string) -- Goodhart-weak:
they pass if the flag STRING is present even when the gating LOGIC is wrong.
These 4 add a BEHAVIOURAL anchor calling the real _should_run_subsystem:
- runs when subsystem in allowed
- skipped when not in allowed
- runs when allowed is None (no budget restriction)
- skipped when allowed is empty set -- the mutation-distinguisher: flipping

subject: feat(relay): opt-out keyed on chat JID + operator-exempt guard + Goodhart tests (#3415)
what: Added opt-out keyed on chat JID + operator-exempt guard + Goodhart tests (#3415).
why:
Fix bug #2139: "GRIP: opt-out" was keyed on sender_id instead of channel_id
(chat_jid), causing an operator-sent opt-out in a contact's chat to tombstone

subject: feat(precog-idr): Wave C — wire real HMAC/Ed25519 signing (#3417)
what: Added wave C — wire real HMAC/Ed25519 signing (#3417).
why:
Replaces sha256-placeholder with tamper-evident HMAC-SHA256 / Ed25519 signing
via lib.audit_chain primitives. sha256-placeholder now returns DEGRADED (not
VERIFIED) from _verify_node — it is structural-only, not cryptographically
tamper-evident. New records always use _sign_real; legacy records remain readable.

subject: fix(hal): correct-by-construction multi-provider routing + isolate routing from telemetry crash (#3423)
what: Fixed correct-by-construction multi-provider routing + isolate routing from telemetry crash (#3423).
why: parse_model_address hardcoded a known_providers set that omitted qwen, xai, moonshot, zhipu, arcee (all declared in config/providers.yaml). Unknown prefixes fell through to ('', address) and routed to the default provider (groq), 404-ing — HAL Council's own xai+qwen members were silently misrouted. Correct-by-construction fix: routing derives from the LIVE registry (chat() passes set(self._providers)); the module function derives its default from the registry singleton with a static floor fallback, so any provider in providers.yaml is routable without editing this function. Adds Goodhart-proof regression tests: a registered provider absent from any static allowlist must route to itself, and every providers.yaml cloud vendor resolves to itself. 16/16 green. Primary Author: [PERSON-8]

subject: chore(hal): log registry-derive failure in _resolve_known_providers (AGORA #3423) (#3424)
what: Maintained log registry-derive failure in _resolve_known_providers (AGORA #3423) (#3424).
why: AGORA's one suggestion on #3423: the except fell back to the static floor with zero visibility. Now logs at debug; behaviour unchanged (still fail-safe to the floor). 16 registry tests green. Primary Author: [PERSON-8]

subject: fix(memory): route grip_generation through the shared D2-correct instance selector (#3425)
what: Fixed route grip_generation through the shared D2-correct instance selector (#3425).
why:
What this does (plain English): the startup line that shows "Gen N" could
disagree with the memory line about which generation GRIP is on. Both read the
same instance history but picked the "latest" entry two different ways — one
correct, one not — so under concurrent sessions they drifted apart.

subject: fix(memory): make MEMORY.md health checks character-aware (real truncation dimension) (#3426)
what: Fixed make MEMORY.md health checks character-aware (real truncation dimension) (#3426).
why:
What this does (plain English): Claude Code only loads the first ~24.4 KB of
MEMORY.md (the memory index) per session and silently drops the rest. GRIP's
two health checks for MEMORY.md were watching the wrong number — they counted
LINES (warn near 200-300), but the harness truncates on CHARACTERS. A file can
sit at ~195 lines yet hold ~46,000 characters because individual index entries
grew into long multi-line blocks. Result: the index was being silently
truncated on load while every GRIP check said it was fine.

subject: feat(memory): content_addr() — P0 precursor for the HAPPI context memory-chain (#3427)
what: Added content_addr() — P0 precursor for the HAPPI context memory-chain (#3427).
why:
What this does (plain English): adds one pure helper giving an IDR a "content
address" — a sha256 of its meaningful body, EXCLUDING the volatile id + timestamp
+ signature. Two IDRs with identical content but different timestamps now share
one address (git's tree-hash excludes the commit date for exactly this reason).
This is the foundation the context-event memory-chain needs for dedup +
tamper-evident linkage; without it content-addressing is vapour, because
build_idr stamps a wall-clock+random id on every record.

subject: feat(memory): P3 — context memory-chain write/read/verify (context_chain + context_head) (#3428)
what: Added p3 — context memory-chain write/read/verify (context_chain + context_head) (#3428).
why:
GRIP-side of the HAPPI memory-chain (design §4-§5). The chain is a linear
sequence of signed context-delta IDRs; append builds via build_idr (HMAC),
appends to state/precog/context.jsonl, and atomically swaps the HEAD pointer
(state/precog/context-HEAD.txt) via os.replace. read resolves HEAD + traverses;
verify rebuilds the IDR forest (rooted at an exogenous council: anchor) and
returns the tri-state Verdict — VERIFIED for a clean chain, BROKEN when a delta
byte is tampered (the integrity anchor). read_latest() is the single reader shim
(design §7).

subject: feat(memory): P4 - context snapshot + deterministic fold bridge (#3429)
what: Added p4 - context snapshot + deterministic fold bridge (#3429).
why:
Bridge the signed context-delta chain into the bounded memory kernel via the
existing holistic_synthesiser._assemble reducer (no second fold, no synthesiser
refactor). A context-snapshot IDR pins kernel_sha256 (payload_digest of the fold
over the pre-snapshot chain - re-derivable, R2 idempotency) + substrate_digest +
folded_nodes (what P5's reaper may reap once the snapshot verifies).
read_context_chain gains an explicit head_pointer param so snapshot_chain folds
the correct chain.

subject: feat(memory): P5 - context GC reaper + ledger-fold (#3430)
what: Added p5 - context GC reaper + ledger-fold (#3430).
why:
Fail-closed reaper (instance #2 of the mesh_gc.py pattern; the generic
ttl_reaper is deferred to N=3 per knowledge-maturation-functor). Reap the
context-delta prefix before the newest VERIFIED snapshot to a cold archive
(recoverable by content address, NEVER hard-deleted) - all-or-nothing, and
fail-closed on any un-snapshotted exogenous anchor in the prefix. Report-split
counts (live vs archived-collapsed) - the grip-session-mesh "1342 waiting"
stale-frame lesson.

subject: fix(caffeinate): grip-caffeinate uses -dimsu (keep display + system awake for WA/HAL)
what: Fixed grip-caffeinate uses -dimsu (keep display + system awake for WA/HAL).
why:
Bare -i let the display sleep (PreventUserIdleDisplaySleep=0), which V>>
linked to WhatsApp listener + HAL gateway drop-outs when away from the Mac.
-dimsu keeps display + system + disk awake and asserts user activity, so the
local WA daemon (:3101) and HAL gateway (:4010) stay connected. Drop -d to
let only the screen sleep.

subject: test(ff-ops): pin CLAUDE_DIR during collection-time kernel exec
what: Added tests for pin CLAUDE_DIR during collection-time kernel exec.
why:
test_ff_ops_cli.py execs bin/ff-ops at module/collection time; bin/ff-ops
resolves lib/ff_ops.py from $CLAUDE_DIR. Sibling test modules (test_backlog_*)
set CLAUDE_DIR to a tmp dir at import scope and collect first (b<f), so the
exec saw a tmp CLAUDE_DIR with no kernel -> sys.exit() -> fatal pytest
INTERNALERROR aborting the whole session. Pin CLAUDE_DIR to the real repo
root for the exec only, then restore the prior value so the polluting
modules' own tests are unaffected. Regression anchor: backlog+ff_ops
collected together stay green (77 passed).

subject: fix(relay): co-steerer read scope is machine-wide, not GRIP-repo-only
what: Fixed co-steerer read scope is machine-wide, not GRIP-repo-only.
why:
Craig's WA relay replied 'my read scope this turn is the GRIP repo only' and
could not find ~/Downloads/PRD.md because the listener prompt framed read access
as 'this repo (~/.claude)'. The co-steerer tool tier (Read/Grep/Glob) reaches
absolute paths anywhere on the machine; the PROMPT was the behavioural limit.
Broaden the framing generically (no machine-path leak): read wherever the request
points across the operator's machine, and never reply 'I can't find it / outside
my repo' when the file lives in another folder — widen the search. Pairs with the
GRIP_RELAY_COSTEERER_WRITE go-live. File also carries the prior prospect-handoff
section. Primary Author: [PERSON-8]

subject: feat(create): /create skill + loop-priority-over-theatre rule (anti-theatre crystallisation)
what: Added /create skill + loop-priority-over-theatre rule (anti-theatre crystallisation).
why:
/create crystallises a PROVEN achievement into the MINIMAL reusable substrate
artifact (skill/agent/rule/playbook/workflow), gated by knowledge-maturation-functor
(N>=3) + an anti-theatre/exogenous criterion as its KERNEL — it refuses more than it
emits, measures by external use not artifact count. First dogfood output:
rules/loop-priority-external-over-theatre.md — autonomous loops bias to EXTERNAL
validation; internal machinery is theatre-until-proven. Sibling to exogenous-anchor-law
(validity vs priority). Both carry falsifiers; both reversible. Origin: V>> 2026-06-07
auto-rsi; the rule's own N>=3 = HAL council + gentner-enforcer + V>>'s teardown.

subject: feat(autonomy): proactive propose-not-act InfiniteMachine tick (default OFF)
what: Added proactive propose-not-act InfiniteMachine tick (default OFF).
why:
Closes the verified reactive->proactive gap: GRIP's substantive 'autonomous on
V's behalf' was interactive-LLM-when-prompted + memory-prose (verified, not
claimed). This adds the PROACTIVE half — a schedulable tick that loads the
operator's persisted goal, asks HAL (economic) to PROPOSE the next EXTERNAL-value
step (governed by loop-priority-external-over-theatre), and reports to V's WhatsApp.

subject: feat(autonomy): proactive tick = balanced act-safe/escalate-gated + generalised + closed-loop AGORA merge
what: Added proactive tick = balanced act-safe/escalate-gated + generalised + closed-loop AGORA merge.
why:
Operator directives (V>> 2026-06-07): (1) not propose-only — ACT on the safe/
reversible envelope, ESCALATE only the genuinely-gated; (2) generalisable core
substrate, not attorney-hardcoded; (3) merge-to-main on an autonomous loop is
AGORA-ratified after CI convergence (exogenous-anchor-law RSI carve-out), NOT a
V>> escalation. Now: domain-agnostic prompt; goal auto-sourced from GRIP next_up
(override file optional); act-mode spawns a capability-locked (Read,Grep,Glob,
Write,Edit,Bash) executor whose dangerous tool calls are blocked by the existing
PreToolUse gate stack; the executor opens PRs → CI+AGORA ratify → merge (closed-
loop). ESCALATE set = non-PR irreversible/destructive, external comms, commercial/
pricing, direction forks, bootstrap-authority PRs. 10 Goodhart-resistant tests.

subject: feat(hal): always-on HAL substrate health probe (prove Sentinel+RAILLM, not claim)
what: Added always-on HAL substrate health probe (prove Sentinel+RAILLM, not claim).
why:
verify-canonical applied to the economic substrate's uptime: a scheduled probe
verifies RAILLM/HAL-infer responds + Sentinel state is healthy, logs every result
to logs/hal-health.jsonl, and alerts the operator ONLY on a healthy->down edge
(no spam). CBC: probe+log+notify-operator only; 6 Goodhart tests. Fills the gap
V>> named ('prove Sentinel + RAILLM functioning always') via /create discipline.

subject: feat(hal): always-on hal-health launchd template (30min monitor)
what: Added always-on hal-health launchd template (30min monitor).
why: Primary Author: [PERSON-8]

subject: feat(memory): context memory-chain write half — per-/save signed deltas + shadow completeness + closer (Slices A/B/C) (#3431)
what: Added context memory-chain write half — per-/save signed deltas + shadow completeness + closer (Slices A/B/C) (#3431).
why: Wire the read-from-previous memory chain's WRITE half. context_chain.checkpoint() writes one signed context-delta per /save, threading its predecessor and carrying the volatile next_step BY VALUE; the first call seeds the chain (genesis). A context-snapshot is cut every 8 deltas (deterministic, replay-stable, never wall-clock). Harness-neutral: no HAL/LLM/Claude-Code import — any HAPPI harness invokes checkpoint() on its own cadence.

subject: feat(precog): failure-prevention engine — predict the context wall, fire relief before it (#3432)
what: Added failure-prevention engine — predict the context wall, fire relief before it (#3432).
why:
Third pole of the gate-RSI dialectic (V>> 2026-06-08): poles 1 (runtime
modification) + 2 (RSI engineering) act AT/AFTER failure (cure); this acts BEFORE
(prevention). Wires the existing copilot precognition substrate —
PredictionRegistry (adaptive_engine) + AdaptiveLearning (adaptive) — it does not
rebuild it (GRIP-First / DRY).

subject: fix(gate): context gate observes-and-continues at the ceiling — never HALT (any mode) (#3433)
what: Fixed context gate observes-and-continues at the ceiling — never HALT (any mode) (#3433).
why:
V>> 2026-06-08 mandate: "DO NOT HALT AT 85% — CONTINUE". The interactive HALT

subject: feat(memory): W1 — deterministic replay (pure re-fold, provider-agnostic) (#3434)
what: Added w1 — deterministic replay (pure re-fold, provider-agnostic) (#3434).
why: Implements replay_deterministic (was a NotImplementedError stub) as PURE re-derivation from the signed chain — NOT LLM cross-provider re-execution (the stub's framing): stochastic LLM output can never be byte-identical, and re-execution would put an LLM on the audit path (the import-isolation lint forbids it). Replay is the deterministic fold over already-signed data — which is WHY it is byte-stable + provider-agnostic.

subject: feat(session): pre-launch session-isolation wizard — worktree guard for concurrent sessions (#3438)
what: Added pre-launch session-isolation wizard — worktree guard for concurrent sessions (#3438).
why:
What this does (plain English)
------------------------------
~/.claude IS the GRIP git-crypt repo. When you run two `claude` sessions at
once, they share one working tree and therefore one git HEAD. The moment any
session runs `git checkout`, it flips the branch for BOTH sessions — silently
contaminating each other's work. This is the single biggest git-friction
source (seen in Gen 348 and Gen 509; Rule 13).

subject: chore(state): separate per-machine runtime writes from the tracked tree (#3440)
what: Maintained separate per-machine runtime writes from the tracked tree (#3440).
why:
What this does (plain English)
------------------------------
The GRIP repo (~/.claude) was always showing as "dirty" — every session,
background daemons and hooks wrote into files that git was tracking, so
`git status` was never clean. A perpetually-dirty tree makes every branch
operation (worktree adds, rebases, checkouts) fraught. This is the deeper
root cause under the shared-tree friction.

subject: fix(ff-ops): record_id is real keccak-256, not NIST SHA3-256 (#2021) (#3449)
what: Fixed record_id is real keccak-256, not NIST SHA3-256 (#2021) (#3449).
why:
ff_ops.record_id() claimed "sha3_256 == keccak256 for ASCII labels" — false.
NIST SHA3-256 (padding 0x06) and Ethereum keccak-256 (padding 0x01) produce a
DIFFERENT digest for EVERY input, so the Python tooling's record ids never
matched the on-chain recordId the FinancialDataRegistry derives via Solidity
keccak256(bytes(label)) — a silent interop bug.

subject: P6 — warm-start reads the signed memory-chain (BOOTSTRAP-GATED · human-merge only · DO NOT auto-merge) (#3441)
what: P6 — warm-start reads the signed memory-chain (BOOTSTRAP-GATED · human-merge only · DO NOT auto-merge) (#3441).

from the signed context memory-chain (P0-P5), with a byte-identical
fallback to today's legacy read whenever no chain exists or it is broken.

subject: feat(idr): Merkle inclusion proofs on the completeness commitment (F3 memory-axis gap) (#3453)
what: Added merkle inclusion proofs on the completeness commitment (F3 memory-axis gap) (#3453).
why:
Wires the RFC6962 Merkle primitive (#3442) into context_snapshot's SHADOW completeness
commitment. paramount_root was a flat SHA256(sorted addrs) — now an RFC6962 Merkle root,
so a SINGLE paramount fact's membership is provable with an O(log N) inclusion proof
WITHOUT revealing the other facts (selective disclosure — the DONNA regulator-disclosure
foundation; closes the F3 memory-axis gap from drafts/happi-fractal-deliberation-verdict.md).

subject: fix(meet-intel): harden /signup Slack-parse guard per AGORA #3459 review (#3461)
what: Fixed harden /signup Slack-parse guard per AGORA #3459 review (#3461).
why:
Follow-up converging the AGORA REQUEST_CHANGES on #3459 (Speaker C):
- Guard the ENTIRE 'end' callback, not just JSON.parse. The callback runs async,
  so ANY synchronous throw in it (not only the parse) escapes the Promise and
  crashes the public /signup process. An outer try/catch now rejects on any
  unexpected throw (statusCode access, snippet build, etc.).
- Sanitize the untrusted Slack body in the error message: strip control chars /
  newlines and cap length (charCodeAt filter, pure-ASCII source — no raw control
  bytes) so a hostile 5xx page can't inject fake log lines or inflate logs.

subject: fix(dispatch-logger): real locked append, drop false-"atomic" dead-tmp dance (#3463)
what: Fixed real locked append, drop false-"atomic" dead-tmp dance (#3463).
why:
_atomic_append's docstring claimed atomic multi-writer appends but wrote a DEAD
mkstemp tmp (never used for the append) then a plain UNLOCKED open('a') buffered
append — interleavable across GRIP's 5+ concurrent sessions for lines > PIPE_BUF,
and an honesty bug (claimed a guarantee it did not provide).

subject: fix(insights): lock dedup read+append in ingest_learnings — TOCTOU dup race (#3462) (#3464)
what: Fixed lock dedup read+append in ingest_learnings — TOCTOU dup race (#3462) (#3464).
why:
ingest_learnings read the existing-ids set then appended new learnings with no
lock — two concurrent sessions could BOTH pass the dedup check before either
appended, writing duplicate rows into learning/insights.jsonl (a real race across
GRIP's 5+ live sessions). Now the read+append run under a sidecar FileLock; also
dedups within the batch (prior code wrote same-id-twice). Best-effort: unlocked
fallback if the lock lib is unavailable (prior behaviour preserved).

subject: fix(precog): unique mkstemp tmp for shared state writes (#3462 #6) (#3465)
what: Fixed unique mkstemp tmp for shared state writes (#3462 #6) (#3465).
why:
write_current_idr + _write_flag_window used a FIXED `.json.tmp` (derived from
CURRENT_IDR_PATH / FLAG_WINDOW_PATH) for their tmp+replace. The precog-orchestrate
hook runs PER SESSION, so across GRIP's 5+ live sessions two writers to the same
shared state/precog/*.json clobber the same tmp mid-write → a half-written tmp can
be os.replace'd into place. (The original triage "likely single-orchestrator" was
wrong — a per-session UserPromptSubmit hook is multi-writer on a shared path.)

subject: fix(agora): ground reviews on distinctive module stems (removes 3x false-fail on small PRs) (#3467)
what: Fixed ground reviews on distinctive module stems (removes 3x false-fail on small PRs) (#3467).
why:
The L3 grounding check flagged each graded speaker on a multi-file PR that did not
contain a diff PATH or basename-with-extension. But speakers cite the MODULE name
("decision_record", "merkle"), not "decision_record.py" — so clean multi-file PRs
false-failed AGORA validity. Observed 3x on 2026-06-08 (#3436, #3453, #3454 first runs).

subject: fix(gates): atomic state-sidecar writes for context-gate + evaluator (#3462 #1/#2) (#3466)
what: Fixed atomic state-sidecar writes for context-gate + evaluator (#3462 #1/#2) (#3466).
why:
V>>-approved. Both gates run as PreToolUse hooks on every tool call / every Read
across 5+ concurrent sessions, and both persisted state via a bare
STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(...)) — which truncates the file mid-write. A
concurrent reader in another session could read the truncated file:
- context_gate.get_state() hits the except and returns the DEFAULT, silently
  resetting warning_issued / triggered_checkpoints (safety-state reset);
- evaluator._load() resets, wiping all sessions' read counters.

subject: feat(idr): Merkle inclusion proofs over the IDR forest (DONNA selective disclosure) (#3469)

the actual decision forest.

subject: feat(relay): WhatsApp group scoped allowlist — sender-scoped group admission (#3485)
what: Added whatsApp group scoped allowlist — sender-scoped group admission (#3485).
why:
A WhatsApp group is a multi-participant untrusted-content surface. The global
SenderAllowlist (allowlist.ts) authenticates a sender but is group-agnostic, so
any allowlisted sender who addresses GRIP in ANY group GRIP belongs to could
drive it. This adds the missing GROUP scope so group-driven GRIP is restricted
to listed groups AND a per-group sender_allow, without widening 1:1 DM access
and without touching the capability lock (--tools).
